Campus Amenities
Discover our world-class facilities and student amenities
1 / 2
Green Campus
We proudly recieved “Swatch Campus Award “.The green campus of the College holds a healthy medicinal garden which is a collection of varities of plants having high medicinal values. We can also see plant species which are very rare.
1 / 2
Transportation
College is providing transportation facilities to various localities. There are total 21 buses for the college.
Board Room
Excellent A/C Room for Executive meeting
Multimedia Conference Room
Well equipped A/C Conference Room with a capacity of 150 occupants for executive meetings and video conferences.
Snacks Bar
We have three snacks bar which are located at bio block, indoor stadium and near to the canteen.
1 / 3
Auditorium
We have a well-built auditorium with a maximum occupancy of 3000. All major programmes of college are conducted in this auditorium.
1 / 2
Fitness Center
A student not only want to be mentally strong but also he/she want to be physically fit. There are separate well equipped physical fitness centre for boys and girls. Proper guidance is provided by the fitness trainers .Its open at both morning and evening.
Reprographic Cetnter
We have two Reprographic centres in the college campus which are located at Bio Block, Decennial block and at Auditorium.
1 / 2
Canteen
In Sahrdaya we have a clean and hygenic canteen that serves food for students as well as faculty members. We have a proper menu of food which is specific for each day. Fixed time schedule is also provided for food delivery.
1 / 3
Hostel
Three hostels named Shalome, Jyothis and Thejus are allotted for girls and Santhome hostel for boys.
We have a separate PG hostel block too. All the hostels are ragging free and it’s truly a home away from home.
Chapel

In order to  enhance the spirituality among Sahrdayans we have a chapel . Every day the holy mass is being celebrated in the chapel under the guidance of Fathers and Sisters. We also conduct holy rosary everyday and adorations on every Mondays and Thursday
ATM
In house South Indian Bank ATM, so that Sahrdayans can withdraw can at any convenient time without any hassle.

About Us
Discover our institution's story and values
Institution Overview
Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology,
Kodakara
Established by the Diocese of Irinjalakuda, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology
was founded with the vision of imparting value-based, quality technical education that
integrates professional competence with ethical responsibility. The institution is managed by
the Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust, registered in 2001, under the visionary leadership
of His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil, the first Bishop of the Diocese.
The college was established in 2002 with approval from the All India Council for Technical
Education (AICTE) and was initially affiliated to the University of Calicut. Located on a
picturesque 25-acre green campus at Kodakara, Thrissur District, the institution provides a
serene and intellectually stimulating environment conducive to academic excellence,
innovation, and holistic development.
In alignment with the restructuring of technical education in the State, the institution
transitioned in 2015 to affiliation with APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University
(APJAKTU), Kerala. Over the years, Sahrdaya has expanded its academic portfolio to offer six
undergraduate (B.Tech) programmes:
 Biomedical Engineering
 Biotechnology Engineering
 Civil Engineering
 Computer Science and Engineering
 Electronics and Communication Engineering
 Electrical and Electronics Engineering
The institution also offers four postgraduate (M.Tech) programmes:
 Biomedical Engineering
 Computer Science and Engineering
 Industrial Biotechnology
 Embedded Systems
In addition to undergraduate and postgraduate programmes, the institution offers PhD research
programmes in approved disciplines, fostering a strong research culture a

nd enabling scholars
to pursue advanced studies aligned with contemporary technological and societal needs. The
college also offers Postgraduate Diploma programmes in emerging and interdisciplinary areas,
aimed at enhancing professional competencies, upskilling graduates, and supporting lifelong
learning in response to evolving industry and research demands.
In recognition of its academic maturity and governance practices, Academic Autonomy was
conferred by the University Grants Commission (UGC) in 2024. This milestone empowered
the institution to implement Outcome-Based Education (OBE), the Choice-Based Credit
System (CBCS), and flexible, industry-aligned curricula, promoting interdisciplinary learning,
innovation, and skill development.
Sahrdaya is NAAC-accredited, and all undergraduate engineering programmes are NBA
accredited by 2025, reflecting sustained adherence to national quality benchmarks. The
institution is well regarded among engineering colleges in Kerala for its disciplined academic
environment, consistent student outcomes, and strong value system.
The college provides state-of-the-art laboratories, ICT-enabled classrooms, a fully digitized
central library, a language laboratory, modern computing facilities, well-maintained hostels,
transport services, and comprehensive sports and fitness infrastructure. The lush green campus
and eco-friendly initiatives contribute to a vibrant and enriching campus life.
Graduates of Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology have progressed to reputed
industries, higher education programmes, research institutions, and entrepreneurial ventures,
reflecting the institution’s sustained emphasis on academic rigour, professional competence,
and lifelong learning. The curriculum design, skill-oriented learning practices, an

d industry
exposure collectively support students in building successful and meaningful career pathways.
Rooted in its founding values, Sahrdaya emphasizes holistic education, nurturing intellectual,
physical, emotional, moral, and spiritual growth. Through value education, community service,
mentoring, and inclusive campus practices, the institution fosters ethical leadership, social
responsibility, compassion, and integrity, while remaining open and welcoming to students
from all backgrounds.
Aligned with its Vision 2030, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology aspires to
emerge as a centre of excellence in autonomous engineering education, with strengthened focus
on research, innovation, entrepreneurship, industry collaboration, global engagement, and
sustainable development, shaping future-ready engineers and responsible global citizens.

Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology - Leading Engineering Institution Kerala
Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology (SCET) has achieved remarkable recognition as one of the top 15 engineering colleges in Kerala, moving up from rank 24 to rank 15 in 2025. This autonomous engineering college maintains NAAC B+ accreditation and NBA accreditation.
Located in Kodakara, Thrissur, Kerala, with 1800+ students, 180+ faculty, 90%+ placement rate. Programs: Computer Science Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Biotechnology, Biomedical Engineering, AI & ML, Data Science, Cyber Security. Affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University. Best engineering college Thrissur, Kerala autonomous KEAM GATE admission 2026.

Management
Meet our management team and organizational structure
Irinjalakuda Diosesan Educational Trust
Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust was registered on 23-07-2001 with Reg. No: 138/IV 2001. The trust belongs to the Catholic Diocese of Irinjalakuda, Christian (Syro-Malabar) minority community. The Registered Office of the Trust is located at Catholic Bishop’s House, Irinjalakuda – 680121, Manavalassery Village, Mukundapuram Taluk , Thrissur District , Kerala State , India.
Patron & Chairman Rt. Rev. Bishop Dr. Pauly Kannookadan Bishop Of Catholic Diocese Of Irinjalakuda
Human Behavior Is A Very Broad And Complex Phenomenon, Involving Numerous Components. Each Individual Possesses A Different ‘Package’ Of Experiences, Values, Needs, Goals, Persuasions And Ideas That Cause Him To Behave Differently From Another. The Task Of Education Is To Change The Behavior. For This, Keep Up The Spirit Of Prayer And Hard Work And Remain Well Disciplined. Mutual Trust And Co-Operation Alone Will Help The Institution To Go Ahead With Full Thrust As One Soul And One Heart, Thereby Making The Venture A Reality.
Manager Msgr. Wilson Erathara
Human mind is a complex unit and all knowledge is interdependent. In a sense, every study should excite and satisfy different mental powers. It must impart intellectual vision, aesthetic enjoyment and practical power to the students. The prime thrust of the diocese is the total transformation of the individual through education by emphasizing the family as the cornerstone of social structure. Intension of our institution is to fabricate each individual as God-fearing and honest citizens, respectful and loving all.

Profile
View detailed institutional profile and achievements
Institutional Profile
Irinjalakuda Diocese was established in the year 1978, and His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil took over the reins as the first Bishop of this Diocese. Irinjalakuda Diocese is doing a excellent service meeting the needs of Education, Health and Social responsibilities. Considering the demands on the necessity of value based higher technical education, the people of the Diocese decided to establish an Engineering college to mark The Episcopal Silver Jubilee Year of His Excellency Bishop Mar James Pazhayattil and Silver Jubilee of the formation of the Diocese of Irinjalakuda .The Irinjalakuda Diocesan Educational Trust was formed and registered on 23-07-2001 with Reg. No: 138/IV 2001 at Irinjalakuda registration office with His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil as Chairman, Rev. Msgr. Sebastian Ezhekadan as President, Rev. Fr. Joseph Thekkethala as Secretary, Rev.Fr. Pius Chirapanath as Finance Officer. With land holding about 40 acres , the Trust submitted the application form for establishing Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology at Kodakara, to the Government of Kerala, University of Calicut and AICTE , New Delhi through the Regional Office. By the Grace of God, the AICTE, New Delhi in its letter dated 06-06-2002, sanctioned the establishment of Sahrdaya College at Kodakara, to run the four B. Tech Degree courses leading to Electronics and Communication Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Biomedical Engineering and Biotechnology (Engineering) with an intake of 60 students in each of the courses from the academic year 2002-2003.
The Government of Kerala included the Sahrdaya College as one of the engineering colleges to which the students have to be admitted by the Com

missioner of Entrance Examination from the year 2002-2003 in the four branches sanctioned by the AICTE. On 21st November 2001 His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil laid the foundation stone for central building of Sahrdaya College. Architects Mayphils from Ernakulam prepared the master plan for the college and detail plan for the individual buildings. On 22/02/2002 His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil laid the foundation stone for the workshop and hostel building in the campus. In a short span of seven months the central building, workshop and hostel block were completed in all respects. All the 4 class rooms of the first batch were equipped with Networked Computers with LCD projector for Multimedia Presentations, Teak wood furniture, imported green glass Magnetic chalk boards etc. The laboratories required for first year in Physics and Chemistry departments and the workshop required in Mechanical, Electrical & Electronics and Civil engineering departments were fully equipped. A drawing hall containing modern adjustable drawing boards was also made available for use. An advanced Computer Lab with 60 networked Pentium-4 terminals was established. A language lab to train the students in English, Hindi, French, German, Arabic and Japanese languages was also established with necessary audio support from Linguaphone Institute Ltd, London. A vast reference library which can accommodate 150 students at a time and issue library with text books and reference books was established with a well qualified librarian and necessary supporting staff. Library is being digitalized. Well-qualified teaching staff varies from master’s degree to doctorate degree conversant in using power point presentations were recruited. Internet facilities are provided in the college. Buses were provided fo

r transporting staff and students. On 22/6/2002, the college building was blessed by His Excellency Mar James Pazhayattil, Honourable M.L.A.’s viz., Adv. K.P. Viswanathan MLA, Mr. P.P. George MLA, Mr. T.U. Radhakrishnan MLA, Ms. Savithri Lakshmanan, MLA, Mr. M.K. Paulson MLA, Mr. P.R. Prasadan, Village Panchayat President, Rev. Priests, Sisters and Parish Representatives, well wishers and other dignitaries were present on the occasion. The college office started functioning in the new premises with Executive Director, Principal and office staff on 23-09-2002. foundation stone for the workshop and hostel building in the campus. Vision & Mission
Our Commitment to Excellence
Discover our vision, mission, and quality policy that guide our pursuit of academic excellence and innovation.
Vision
Evolve as a leading technology institute to create high caliber leaders and innovators of global standing with strong ethical values to serve the industry and society.
Mission
Provide quality technical education that transforms students into knowledgeable, skilled, innovative, and entrepreneurial professionals.
Collaborate with academia and industry worldwide to strengthen the education and research ecosystem.
Uphold and promote professional ethics, discipline, integrity, and social responsibility with a passion for holistic excellence.
Quality Policy
At Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, we are committed to delivering quality technical education through continuous improvement and value-based learning.
We aim to mould vibrant engineers with high professional standards by providing:
At Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, we are committed to delivering quality technical education through continuous improvement and value-based learning.
We aim to mould vibrant engineers with high professional standards by providing:
Competent and dedicated faculty
Excellent infrastructure and learning environment
Active collaboration with industries to foster an ambience of excellence

Announcements
Official announcements and notices
Data Science Club Inaguration
1 week ago
The Data Pulse – Data Science Club of Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology is being inaugurated to create a vibrant platform for students to explore the rapidly evolving field of data science and artificial intelligence. The club aims to foster innovation, analytical thinking, and practical learning through workshops, expert talks, competitions, and collaborative projects.
The inauguration ceremony will feature an invited talk by Prof. Dr. Sabu M K, Professor, Department of Computer Applications, Cochin University of Science and Technology, on the topic “Retrieval Augmented Generation: Enhancing LLMs with External Data.” The session will provide valuable insights into emerging AI technologies and their real-world applications.
Logo Design Competition
1 week ago
​🚀 Calling all Designers! 🚀
​The Data Science Club is hosting a Digital Logo Design Competition! We invite students from Semesters 2, 4, and 6 to showcase their creativity.
​The Challenge: Design a logo reflecting intelligence, innovation, and technology.
​The Prize: The winning design becomes our Official Logo, and the winner will be awarded!
​Show us your vision for the future of Data Science!
Session on REGULATORY COMPLIANCE FOR MEDICAL DEVICES: FDA, ISO, IEC, AND ICMED STANDARDS
1 week ago
Department of Biomedical Engineering in association with NDLI and EBSA is conducting a session on REGULATORY COMPLIANCE FOR MEDICAL DEVICES: FDA, ISO, IEC, AND ICMED STANDARDS by Ms. Mary Roopsy Joy (Alumni 2002 Batch), Quality Assurance and Regulatory Affairs Expert for Medical Devices on 9th March 2026.
Becoming Her
1 week ago
The Women’s Innovation Club of Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Centre at Sahrdaya Coll

ege of Engineering & Technology presents “Becoming Her”, a three-day online interactive talk series as part of Women’s Day celebrations.
The sessions explore inspiring female characters and the qualities that shape confidence, resilience, and leadership.
Herwall
1 week ago
As part of the Women’s Day celebrations, the Women Innovation Club invites students to participate in Her Wall—a space to celebrate the women who inspire us. Paste a picture of a woman who has influenced your life and be part of honoring their impact.
She speaks 2.0
1 week ago
She Speaks 2.0 is a debate competition organized as part of the Women’s Day celebrations, providing a platform for students to express their ideas, debate on thought-provoking topics, and showcase their speaking skills.
🔋 Smarter Energy for Modern Living  ( A Talk on Energy Management & Today’s Energy Scenario )
2 weeks ago
📢 IEEE Control Systems Society – Sahrdaya SB presents
🔋 Smarter Energy for Modern Living
( A Talk on Energy Management & Today’s Energy Scenario )
📅 5 March 2026 (Thursday)
⏰ 10:00 AM – 12:30 PM
🎓 For 2nd & 3rd Year EEE Students
The IEEE Control Systems Society – Sahrdaya Student Branch, in association with the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering, is organizing an insightful technical talk titled “Smarter Energy for Modern Living” on 5th March 2026, exclusively for Second and Third Year EEE students.
This session focuses on one of the most significant domains in modern electrical engineering — Energy Management and the Present Energy Scenario at both national and global levels.
About the Speaker
Adil Nasser
Assistant Professor, College of Engineering Trikaripur
State Coordinator, APJ KTU NSS Energy Cell
Gain insights into the current energy scenario, renewable integration, and energy manageme

nt practices from an expert in the field.

AGM & Award Day 2026
2 weeks ago
The IEEE Sahrdaya Student Branch is proud to host its Annual General Meeting (AGM) & Award Day 2026.
We are honoured to welcome Mr. S. Subramanian, Founding Architect of IRCTC and Bombay Stock Exchange systems, as our Chief Guest for this prestigious occasion.
This event celebrates the achievements of our EXECOM members and marks the official leadership transition for the year ahead.
📌 Event Details:
🗓 Date: 27th February 2026
⏰ Time: 2:00 PM onwards
📍 Venue: Multi-Purpose Auditorium
🎟 Exclusively for: EXECOM Members
Let’s gather to celebrate excellence, leadership, and new beginnings ✨

Voltx Workshop
1 month ago
"The Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering, in association with the project te

am SCET VAIDYUTH, is conducting a workshop titled VOLT X from 4th February to 27th February 2026 for S2 students.
The workshop aims to provide basic-level technical training related to the development of a solar-powered autonomous electric vehicle. The training will include an introduction to the software tools used in the project, namely MATLAB and TinkerCAD, along with fundamental electrical concepts such as wiring practices, electrical components, types of motors, and their specifications. Students will also be introduced to the mechanical works and integrations involved in the project, giving them an overall understanding of how electrical, software, and mechanical systems are combined in a real-time application.
Registration Fee : 30 Rs/-
Registration link : https://forms.gle/fGjGobv49h4VWxET9
The faculty in charge for the workshop are Dr. V. Vijikala and Sreekanth S, Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering."

Now Hiring: Leadership & Faculty Positions
We are seeking dynamic professionals across all departments for the following roles:
I. Strategic Leadership Opportunities (Deans)
We seek transformative leaders for the following portfolios to drive our institutional vision:
Academic Excellence
Dean (UG) | Dean (PG)
Research & Innovation
Dean (Research) | Dean (Consultancy and Industry Connect)
Operations & Growth
Dean (Administration) | Dean (Planning)
Student Success
Dean (Student Affairs) | Dean (Career Guidance, Training, and Placement)
II. Faculty Positions
Senior Professor | Professor | Associate Professor | Assistant Professor
Departments & Areas of Specialization
Department
Areas of Specialization & Research
Industry & Consultancy Verticals
AI / ML & Computer Science
Generative & Agentic AI: LLMs, RAG, Transformers, Few-shot learning. Future Tech: Quantum ML/Cryptography, Edge Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Affective Computing.
AI Ethics auditing, MLOps, Data Engineering, UI/UX Design, and AI-integrated Full-stack development.
Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS)
Smart Systems: Real-time systems (RTOS), IoT/IIoT, Sensor Fusion, LPWAN (5G/6G), and Digital Twins. Security: Cyber Forensics, Zero-trust Network Security.
Industrial Automation (Industry 4.0), Smart City infrastructure, and Secure Network protocols.
Electronics
Silicon Engineering: Advanced VLSI/ASIC Design, FPGA-based Systems, Signal & Image Processing, Low-power IC Design for Wearables.
Hardware Security, SoC (System on Chip) prototyping, and Hardware-level encryption services.
Biotechnology
Modern Bio-Eng: Computational/Systems Biology, Synthetic Biology, Biopharmaceuticals, Bioprocess Engineering.
Bio-process optimization, industrial fermentation, and bio-fuel research.
Biomedical Engineering
Med

-Tech: Biosensor fabrication, Biomechanics, Physiological modelling, Virtual Instrumentation (LabVIEW/Simulink).
CAD/Prototyping of medical devices, diagnostic tool design, and regulatory compliance.
Electrical & Computer Eng.
Energy Systems: EV Charging Infrastructure, Battery Management (BMS), Renewable Energy Integration, AI-driven Control.
Microgrid management, Energy Auditing, and Smart Grid stabilization for industry.
Civil
Resilient Infra: Structural Health Monitoring (SHM), BIM (4D/5D Modeling), Revit architecture, GIS & Remote Sensing.
Green Building Certification (LEED), BIM-led Project Management, and Smart Materials.
Applied Science & Humanities
Foundational Research: Stochastic modeling for AI, Nanomaterials, Ethics in Technology, and Professional Communication.
Mathematical modeling for Finance, Material characterization, and Tech-Societal impact studies.
The Sahrdaya Value Proposition: A Path of Growth and Fulfillment
A Culture of Innovation
:
Active involvement in centres of excellence, product development, and industry-academia collaboration.

PG  Diploma in Data Science and Intelligent Systems
The PG Diploma program is designed as an internationally aligned postgraduate qualification integrating advanced coursework, industry-oriented training, and research-based dissertation components. The curriculum is structured to meet global academic standards and to enable academic progression opportunities through collaborations with leading universities in New Zealand.
Through this collaborative framework, students may be eligible to transfer selected academic credits (subject to evaluation and approval by the partnering universities) after successful completion of the prescribed modules. This enables students to pursue advanced postgraduate qualifications or Master’s programs abroad by completing the remaining requirements at the partner institution.
Students who continue their studies in New Zealand will be awarded degrees by the respective partnering universities upon successful completion of the program requirements. Tuition and admission conditions at the partner institution will be governed by their prevailing regulations.
The PG Diploma is a 6-month intensive program structured around advanced core courses, laboratory components, research methodology, industry certification (such as AI-focused workforce training), and dissertation phases. The program is designed to bridge academic excellence with global industry demands.
In collaboration with select universities in New Zealand, the program creates opportunities for:
Academic articulation pathways
Joint supervision for dissertation work
Exposure to international research ecosystems
Progression into Master’s degree programs
Credits earned during the PG Diploma may be consider

ed for advanced standing by the partnering universities based on their academic policies. Students opting for international progression will complete the remaining coursework or research components at the partner university and will be awarded the final degree by that institution.
The PG Diploma program is designed to foster innovation, applied research, and interdisciplinary expertise. With a structured combination of coursework, laboratory training, research methodology, and dissertation phases, the program prepares students for advanced research and professional careers.
Through academic partnerships with universities in New Zealand, the program facilitates:
Academic mentoring from international faculty (where applicable)
Exposure to global research practices
Pathways to postgraduate degree continuation
Opportunities for collaborative research initiatives
Students completing the diploma may apply for credit recognition or advanced entry into postgraduate programs offered by the partner universities, subject to their admission and credit transfer policies. Final degrees will be conferred by the respective partnering university upon successful completion.
This collaborative structure promotes:
International academic mobility
Global research collaboration
Enhanced academic credentials
Competitive advantage in higher education and employment sectors
Program Structure
This program is structured at the Postgraduate Diploma level. Students enrolling in the PG Diploma will complete the prescribed coursework, laboratory components, industry-integrated training, and dissertation phases at the home institution. Upon successful completion of the PG Diploma requirements, eligible students may progress to partnering universities in New Zealand (and other collaborating countries),

subject to admission and credit evaluation policies of the host institution.
Students opting for international progression may receive advanced standing or credit recognition, enabling them to complete the remaining academic or research requirements at the host university. Upon successful completion of the prescribed program abroad, the final postgraduate degree will be awarded by the respective partnering university. This structure provides a seamless academic pathway from a PG Diploma to an international Master’s qualification, enhancing global exposure, research opportunities, and international career prospects.
Benefits
•
The program is strengthened through academic collaboration with international partner universities, ensuring exposure to contemporary curricular developments, emerging technologies, and global best practices.
•
By completing the PG Diploma at the home institution before progressing abroad, students substantially reduce the overall cost of international education compared to pursuing a full overseas program.
•
Graduates who continue their studies abroad gain access to international job markets with competitive salary packages, enabling faster recovery of educational investment.
•
Students may gain access to a broader spectrum of advanced electives and specialization areas at partner universities, including niche domains not widely available in India.
•
Studying across two academic systems fosters cross-cultural competence, adaptability, and global professional readiness.
•
The program provides a smooth transition to Master’s degree programs at the partner university or other leading global institutions, with potential eligibility for Teaching Assistantships (TA) or Research Assistantships (RA), thereby reducing the financial burden of higher studies

.

Admission Procedure
Step 1: Apply for PG Diploma at Sahrdaya
Candidates must first apply for admission to the 6-Month Full-Time PG Diploma in Data Science & Intelligent Systems at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology (SCET).
📌 Application Process
Submit online application form
Upload academic documents
Pay application/registration fee
Attend screening/interaction (if applicable)
Admission is based on:
Academic merit in qualifying degree
Eligibility criteria fulfillment
Seat availability
Step 2: Registration for International Progression Pathway (Optional)
Students interested in progressing to Master’s programs at:
Wintec
Wellington Institute of Technology
must register separately with the International Relations / Global Pathways Cell.
The International Office will:
Counsel students regarding suitable Master’s programs
Guide on credit transfer eligibility
Assist with documentation & application submission
Support visa processing guidance
Step 3: Academic Performance Requirement
For international progression, students must:
Successfully complete the PG Diploma
Maintain required academic performance
Meet English language requirements (IELTS/PTE as prescribed by NZ institution)
Fulfill admission criteria of the partner university
Final admission to the NZ university is subject to:
Acceptance by the host institution
Credit recognition evaluation
Seat availability
If a student does not receive admis

sion from the selected NZ university, they may:
Complete the PG Diploma at SCET and receive the diploma certificate
Apply for the next intake
Explore alternate partner options
The PG Diploma admission is independent of NZ university admission.
International progression is optional and subject to eligibility.
Eligibility
Eligibility for Admission at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology (PG Diploma)
•
Direct Admission (No Work Experience Required)
Candidates must have:
B.Tech / BE in Computer Science Engineering (CSE) / Information Technology (IT)
MCA
M.Sc. Computer Science
•
With Minimum 2 Years Relevant Work Experience (IT/Software Domain)
Candidates with the following qualifications are eligible:
B.Tech / BE in Non-CSE branches
B.Sc. Computer Science / IT
BCA / BCS or equivalent
Eligibility for Master’s Progression at New Zealand Institutions
•
Successful completion of the PG Diploma at Sahrdaya with 65% marks
•
English Language Proficiency: IELTS Academic 6.5 overall

HOD Message
About Computer Science & Engineering
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology is committed to excellence in education, research, and service. We prepare students to become leaders and innovators in the rapidly evolving field of computer science and engineering.
Established 2009
NBA Accredited
400+ Students
20+ Faculty
Vision
To be a center of excellence in computer science and engineering education, fostering innovation, research, and ethical leadership to address global technological challenges and contribute to societal development.
Mission
To provide quality education in computer science and engineering through innovative teaching methodologies, cutting-edge research, industry collaboration, and comprehensive skill development to produce competent professionals and entrepreneurs.
Department Overview
The Computer Science and Engineering department offers comprehensive undergraduate and postgraduate programs designed to meet the evolving needs of the technology industry. Our curriculum combines theoretical foundations with practical applications, ensuring students are well-prepared for successful careers in software development, research, and entrepreneurship.
With state-of-the-art laboratories, experienced faculty, and strong industry partnerships, we provide an environment that encourages innovation, critical thinking, and professional growth. Our graduates have consistently demonstrated excellence in various fields including artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, and software engineering.
Key Highlights
Academic Excellence
• NBA accredited

programs
• Updated curriculum aligned with industry needs
• Outcome-based education approach
• Strong placement record
Research & Innovation
• Active research in emerging technologies
• Student research projects
• Industry collaboration
• Publications in reputed journals

Examination Conduct
Guidelines and Regulations
CONDUCT OF EXAMINATIONS & EVALUATION
1 Distribution of Examination Materials
The Examination Materials will be distributed from the Autonomous Examination Cell.
2 Invigilation
Invigilators shall be responsible for the fair conduct of the examination inside the examination hall. After the commencement of the examination, allotted bar codes shall be pasted on the space provided in the answer booklet and attendance sheet. The candidate's attendance shall be recorded by obtaining signature in the attendance sheet provided which shall be countersigned by the invigilator. Invigilators shall ensure that all candidates have returned the answer scripts. The answer scripts shall be handed over to the examination control room within 15 minutes after the examination. In case a candidate is found to have indulged in malpractice, his/her answer script shall be separately handed over to the CoE. Instructions to the Invigilators are given in Annexure I.
3 Collection and Bundling of Answer Scripts
The answer scripts from the examination halls shall be received at the examination control room after each examination. Answer scripts shall be bundled separately for each course and details such as the name of examination, date of examination, packet code, course code and course name, semester and the number of answer scripts shall be entered on the packing slip. The packed and sealed bundles shall be kept in the safe custody of the DyC(Ex) till the despatch to the evaluation camp.
4. Academic Discipline and Malpractices in Examinations
Every student is required to observe discipline and decorous behaviour in the examination hall. Any act of indiscipline, misbehaviour and unfair practice in examinations shall be punishable according to the nature

of offense committed by the student.
5. Post Examination
The major activities involved during this phase includes the collection and verification of answer scripts, distribution of answer scripts to the examiners, valuation of answer scripts by various levels of examiners, providing necessary instructions and monitoring the valuation process, tabulation of marks and return of the valued answer scripts to the camp for revaluation purpose. All the activities of this phase will be under the direct control of CoE and Dyc(Ev).
6. Appointment of Evaluators
●The CoE shall appoint evaluators from the panel of examiners nominated by the departments to evaluate the answer scripts.
●Evaluators shall have handled the course at least once.
●The number of evaluators appointed for each course shall be based on the number of candidates registered.
●There shall be three evaluators for the evaluation in a given term for a given course.
●Communication regarding the details of answer scripts allotted for evaluation shall be sent to evaluators in advance.
7 Valuation Process
1.Initial Evaluation by Two Evaluators:
●All answer scripts will be evaluated by two evaluators.
2.Variation in Marks (≤ 15% of Maximum Marks):
●If the variation in total marks scored between the two evaluations is less than or equal to 15% of the maximum possible marks, the average mark (calculated question-wise) will be considered in the final tabulation of results.
3.Variation in Marks (> 15% of Maximum Marks):
●If the variation exceeds 15% of the maximum possible marks, the answer script will be sent for a third evaluation by an evaluator appointed by the Controller of Examinations (CoE).
●Final Mark Calculation: The final marks will be the average of the two closest scores obtained from the three evaluations. This

calculation will be done question- wise. (*)
8. Post Evaluation Audit
●Post evaluation audit shall be conducted for checking transparency and fairness of the evaluation.
●10% of evaluated answer scripts selected at random shall be audited by the expert.
●Adherence of evaluation to scheme and answer key provided shall be verified.
●Consistency of evaluation among answer scripts for a particular course shall be verified.
●Observations regarding inconsistency in evaluation observed if any shall be reported to the CoE.
9 .Uploading Marks
Marks shall be uploaded by the evaluators and a hard copy from the portal by the evaluator concerned shall be handed over to CoE.
10.Storage of Evaluated Answer Scripts
●The answer scripts after evaluation shall be bundled, properly recorded and kept in safe custody at the evaluation camp for easy retrieval for three months from the date of publication of results and subsequently stored in the strong room for four years. Answer scripts after four years of storage will be disposed of, as per the decisions of the Academic Council (AC).
●The DyC(Ev) shall hand over evaluated answer scripts for re-evaluation or other purposes on- demand within prescribed time limits.
11. Laboratory / Project / Dissertation / Viva Voce
The scheduling of all Practical Examinations shall be provided by the Head of the concerned Departments to the CoE sufficiently in advance of the scheduled commencement of the examinations.
Evaluations in respect of the Laboratory Courses, Seminars, Mini Projects, Projects, Viva Voce examinations, etc., shall be duly completed by internal/external examiners appointed by COE from the panel submitted by the respective HOD. The faculty in charge shall upload the final marks to the portal within five working days after the examinatio
n/viva is conducted.

MOU's & Exchange Programme
IIIC - Strategic Partnerships for Innovation Excellence
IIIC has established strategic partnerships with leading industries, research institutions, and academic organizations to provide comprehensive learning opportunities, industry exposure, and professional development for innovation and entrepreneurship.
Training & Development
Comprehensive skill development and professional training programs
Research & Innovation
Collaborative R&D projects and academic research initiatives
Industry Partnerships
Strategic collaborations with leading organizations
Internship Programs
Hands-on experience and project opportunities for students
Our Partners
32 active MOUs
AI Kyro
The AI Kyro Startup Studio is a bold, university-powered innovation engine where real-world founders team up with curated student talent and faculty mentors to bring AI-first MVPs to life — faster, leaner, and smarter.
Date:
2025-12-15
Academic Year:
2025-2026
Focus:
The purpose of this MOU is to formalize a strategic collaboration to establish an elite innovation program within the College's ecosystem, transforming the institution into a recognized Skill-First Institute while providing exceptional real-world learning opportunities for students and faculty.
Scope:
Foundation Phase: Skill development and team formation with industry exposure , Project Phase: Real startup project assignment with mentorship support , Delivery Phase: MVP development, demo presentation, and career advancement opportunities
Benefits:
AI Kyro Shall Provide: Program Development &Management, Project & Mentorship Services, Financial & Career Benefits, Platform & Technology.
Hykon India Limited
Hykon, Powering the Nation, an ISO 9001 company, is a pioneering presence in the field of power electronics

with varied interests in Medical transcription, Information Technology, Solar Energy and so forth. Mr. Christo George, a committed and dedicated technocrat, set it up in 1991.
Date:
2024-07-05
Academic Year:
2024-2025
Focus:
The focus of the MoU is to practically enhance the technical and professional capabilities of students and faculty by providing expert guidance from Hykon and exposing them to current industry practices. It aims to strengthen the research culture within the institution by promoting collaborative research, consultancy, and technology development initiatives. A key emphasis is on bridging academia and industry by enabling students and faculty to work on real-world problems, participate in industry-based projects, and gain insights from practical industrial environments. The MoU also prioritizes improving student employability through structured internships, extended on-the-job training programs, and the possibility of job placements based on performance and available opportunities. Additionally, the partnership fosters continuous knowledge exchange by organizing workshops, seminars, short-term courses, and conferences in areas of mutual interest, ensuring ongoing learning and professional growth.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU covers a wide range of collaborative activities between Hykon and Sahrdaya, including strengthening industry–academic interaction through joint efforts in academic learning, research, training, and professional development. It extends to shared academic and research activities such as consultancy, product development, and technical projects, along with comprehensive skill development initiatives like internships, on-the-job training, expert lectures, workshops, and other competency-enhancing programs. The partnership also involve

s mutual access to laboratories, testing facilities, and institutional resources on a preferential basis. To ensure effective execution, the MoU establishes coordination cells and a Network Coordination Committee to monitor and manage all activities. The agreement is valid for three years, with provisions for extension, and includes essential legal elements such as confidentiality, financial responsibilities, indemnity, and termination procedures.
Benefits:
The MoU brings significant benefits to both Sahrdaya College and Hykon India Ltd., creating a mutually enriching partnership. For students and faculty at Sahrdaya, it provides enhanced learning opportunities through exposure to industry-grade projects, hands-on training, and real-world applications, along with access to advanced research and product development environments. It strengthens skill development by offering expert mentoring, practical experience, and opportunities for upskilling through workshops and seminars led by Hykon professionals. The agreement also improves career prospects by providing structured internships, on-the-job training, and the possibility of recruitment based on performance, while giving students preferential access to Hykon’s facilities for academic work. For Hykon, the MoU supports a robust R&D pipeline by enabling collaboration with academic researchers and student project teams, fostering new product ideas and technological solutions. It also helps identify talented students for potential employment and offers cost-effective access to Sahrdaya’s facilities and academic expertise. Both parties benefit through shared intellectual property, stronger industry–academic ties, enhanced innovation, and efficient sharing of responsibilities and costs related to research, monitoring, and coor

dination.
View MOU Document →
Kerala Veterinary and Animal Science University
This Memorandum of Understanding establishes a collaborative framework between the two institutions to promote cooperation in research, academics, product development, validation, entrepreneurship, and innovation. The MoU aims to facilitate the sharing of knowledge, technical expertise, and resources to enhance mutual learning and development in science, engineering, medical, veterinary, and allied disciplines. Through joint programs, training opportunities, exchange of data, and organized academic events, both parties will work together to create impactful research outcomes, strengthen educational processes, and support innovation-driven activities.
Date:
2025-09-29
Academic Year:
2024-2025
Focus:
The primary focus of this MoU is to encourage collaboration in research and development, student innovation, and interdisciplinary knowledge sharing. It aims to support joint academic initiatives, exchange of expertise in engineering, medical, and veterinary technologies, and engagement in product development and validation. The MoU also focuses on fostering entrepreneurship, facilitating professional training programs, and supporting innovation ecosystems through cooperation with Technology Business Incubators and startup activities.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU includes joint research activities, exchange of publications and data, and collaborative development programs across engineering, biotechnology, veterinary science, farm mechanization, and healthcare technologies. It also extends to supporting student project works, organizing seminars, workshops, conferences, and short-term programs of mutual interest. Additionally, the MoU covers training opportunities, shared access to expertise and faci

lities, participation in MOOC-based learning, and coordinated arrangements for stakeholder engagement, including startups and innovation centers. Specific collaborative projects will be defined under separate agreements as required.
Benefits:

Kerala Electrical & Allied Engineering Co. Ltd. (KEL)
Established in 1964 in the State of Kerala, India, the Kerala Electrical & Allied Engineering Co. Ltd. (KEL) is a multifaceted company fully owned by the State government. Through it’s four production facilities, located in various districts of the State, this ISO 9001 : 2000 complaint company provides basic engineering services / products besides executing projects of national significance for high profile clients like the various defence establishments. The company manufactures and markets products like general purpose brushless alternators, brushless alternators for lighting and air-conditioning of rail coaches, medium power and distribution transformers as well as structural steel fabrications.
Date:
2024-11-18
Academic Year:
2023-2024
Focus:
The focus of this MoU is to create a strong and sustainable industry–academia interface by promoting collaborative research, knowledge exchange, and skill development between KEL and SCET. The partnership emphasizes the enhancement of research and product development capabilities through joint projects, simulation, design analysis, and consultancy activities. It also focuses on strengthening academic quality by involving industry experts in curriculum development, t

raining programmes, and workshops. Additionally, the MoU prioritizes student growth by providing opportunities for internships, industrial training, mentoring, and exposure to real-world engineering practices. Overall, the focus is on fostering innovation, building technical competency, and enabling meaningful academic–industry integration.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU encompasses a comprehensive range of collaborative academic–industry initiatives aimed at enhancing education, research, and industrial exposure for both KEL and SCET. It includes the joint execution of industry–academic programmes such as research, consultancy, training, and development activities, along with the mutual exchange of facilities, expertise, and technical resources. The partnership also covers joint projects involving simulation, design optimisation, analysis, and product development, supported by faculty collaboration in curriculum development, knowledge sharing, and technical training. Student-focused initiatives—such as internships, industrial training, workshops, and structured mentoring—form a key part of the scope. To ensure smooth implementation and oversight of all activities, a Coordination Cell will be established at both institutions.
Benefits:
The MoU offers significant benefits to both KEL and SCET by enabling shared access to expertise, facilities, and collaborative opportunities. SCET benefits through enhanced academic quality, improved research capabilities, and increased student employability through internships, hands-on training, and mentorship provided by KEL professionals. Faculty members gain through technical training, exposure to industrial practices, and participation in joint research and consultancy projects. KEL benefits from access to academic insights, fresh tal

ent, and innovative solutions emerging from collaborative research and student projects. Both institutions gain through mutual knowledge enhancement, shared intellectual property opportunities, and the establishment of a continuous pipeline of skill development, workshops, and conferences. Overall, the partnership strengthens innovation, productivity, and long-term academic–industry synergy.

Overview
Internal Quality Assurance Cell
To keep up with the changes in higher education, educational Institution must actively engage themselves in the development and implementation of quality benchmarks in all their key performance areas.
Committed to sustain its top ranking position, Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology has established the Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) in 2015 to institutionalize the process of quality enhancement and sustenance in all the key areas of its delivery.
The IQAC is acting as the nodal agency entrusted with the responsibility of ensuring total quality management by ushering in innovations leading to achieving excellence and adoption of intervention strategies for monitoring their successful implementation. Ever in quest for excellence, the IQAC functions to assure institutional accountability with probity to all the stakeholders, funding agencies in particular and society as a whole.
Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC has become a part of the institution’s system and work towards realization of the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the overall performance; which would channelize all efforts and measures of the institution towards promoting its holistic academic excellence.

Functions
Key functions and responsibilities of IQAC
IQAC Functions
1
Development and application of quality benchmarks/parameters for various academic and administrative activities of the institution
2
Facilitating the creation of a learner-centric environment conducive to quality education and faculty maturation to adopt the required knowledge and technology for participatory teaching and learning process
3
Arrangement for feedback response from students, parents and other stakeholders on quality-related institutional processes
4
Dissemination of information on various quality parameters of higher education
5
Organization of inter and intra institutional workshops, seminars on quality related themes and promotion of quality circles
6
Documentation of the various programmes/activities leading to quality improvement
7
Acting as a nodal agency of the Institution for coordinating quality-related activities, including adoption and dissemination of best practices
8
Development and maintenance of institutional database through MIS for the purpose of maintaining/enhancing the institutional quality
9
Development of Quality Culture in the institution
10
Preparation of the Annual Quality Assurance Report (AQAR) as per guidelines and parameters of NAAC, to be submitted to NAAC

Objectives
Core objectives and goals of IQAC
The prime objective of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent & catalytic improvement in the overall performance of institution.
Key Objectives
To develop a system for conscious, consistent, and catalytic action to improve the academic and administrative performance of the institution.
To promote measures for institutional functioning towards quality enhancement through internalization of quality culture and institutionalization of best practices.

NIRF
National Institutional Ranking Framework
NIRF Document
View or download the National Institutional Ranking Framework document
View Document
Download PDF

Quality Initiatives
Quality Initiatives
The Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) enhances institutional
quality through its supportive cells like ISO, RDC, IEDC, IIIC, and
Placement & Training.
IQAC conducts semester and annual planning meetings to align activities with the Strategic Action Plan 2030.
It also organizes ISO management review meetings, ensuring adherence to ISO 9001:2015 QMS standards, where critical areas affecting quality are reviewed, feedback analyzed, and corrective actions implemented.
Key initiatives include the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Cell (IEDC), which earned recognition under Kerala Startup Mission's Local Entrepreneurship Advancement Programme (LEAP) and partnered with NASSCOM Startups.
The Research Innovation Programme for women startups, funded by Kerala Startup Mission with a ₹25 lakh budget, supported 7 women researchers with grants up to ₹5 lakh. Four innovations from the college received recognition at the IEDC Idea Fest 2023.
The Research and Development Cell (RDC) achieved the granting of four patents and the publication of three patents. Additionally, the RDC contributed 30 conference papers and 12 publications. These efforts reflect the institution’s commitment to quality, innovation, and research, fostering a robust environment for academic and entrepreneurial growth.
The IQAC ensures continuous improvement through structured planning and monitoring processes.

Strategies
Overview
add that top text here
Key Strategies
Ensuring timely, efficient and progressive performance of academic, administrative and financial tasks
The relevance and quality of academic and research programmes
Equitable access to and affordability of academic programmes for various sections of society
Optimization and integration of modern methods of teaching and learning
The credibility of evaluation procedures
Ensuring the adequacy, maintenance and functioning of the support structure and services
Research sharing and networking with other institutions in India and abroad

Vision & Mission
Vision
To make quality the defining element of higher education in India through a combination of self and external quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance initiatives.
Mission
To arrange for periodic assessment and accreditation of institutions of higher education or units thereof, or specific academic programmes or projects;
To stimulate the academic environment for promotion of quality of teaching-learning and research in higher education institutions
To encourage self-evaluation, accountability, autonomy and innovations in higher education
To undertake quality-related research studies, consultancy and training programmes, and
To collaborate with other stakeholders of higher education for quality evaluation, promotion and sustenance.

About NDLI Club
Learn about NDLI Club and its mission
NDLI CLUB
National Digital Library of India (NDLI) is a virtual repository of learning resources which is not just a repository with search/browse facilities but provides a host of services for the learner community. It is sponsored and mentored by Ministry of Education, Government of India, through its National Mission on Education through Information and Communication Technology (NMEICT). Filtered and federated searching is employed to facilitate focused searching so that learners can find the right resource with least effort and in minimum time. NDLI provides user group-specific services such as Examination Preparatory for School and College students and job aspirants. Services for Researchers and general learners are also provided. NDLI is designed to hold content of any language and provides interface support for 10 most widely used Indian languages. It is built to provide support for all academic levels including researchers and life-long learners, all disciplines, all popular forms of access devices and differently-abled learners. It is designed to enable people to learn and prepare from best practices from all over the world and to facilitate researchers to perform inter-linked exploration from multiple sources. It is developed, operated and maintained from Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur.
NDLI MEMBERSHIP
Dear Students,
National Digital Library of India (NDLI) is a virtual repository of learning resources
which is not just a repository with search/browse facilities but provides a host of
services for the learner community. It is sponsored and mentored by Ministry of
Education, Govt. of India.
National Digital Library of India Club (NDLI Club) is an enabler of career progression for
students, job seek

ers, researchers and learners. NDLI Clubs are set up in institutes and
nodal bodies, and these Clubs conduct events to facilitate students to develop
knowledge, skill and traits beyond regular curriculum which are essential for their
progression in respective professional domain. The events may be either physical or
virtual. Virtual events are web-based and are conducted through the NDLI Club portal.
The events are organized at respective institute level or city/district/state/regional
level or all-India level.
I request all the students to enrol themselves as member of the NDLI Club. The
link for registration is given below. Use your personal e-mail ID (gmail) and the
Passkey given below.
Link: https://club.ndl.iitkgp.ac.in/sign-up
Passkey: d75b0cc7-7120-46c9-baf0-f250878e719c
Activity Points and Certificate of participation will be awarded
Regards

Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology - Leading Engineering Institution Kerala
Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology (SCET) has achieved remarkable recognition as one of the top 15 engineering colleges in Kerala, moving up from rank 24 to rank 15 in 2025. This autonomous engineering college maintains NAAC B+ accreditation and NBA accreditation.
Located in Kodakara, Thrissur, Kerala, with 1800+ students, 180+ faculty, 90%+ placement rate. Programs: Computer Science Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Biotechnology, Biomedical Engineering, AI & ML, Data Science, Cyber Security. Affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University. News
Latest news and campus updates
TinkerHub Sahrdaya Participates in Impact Meet – Women Makers Celebration 2026
4 days ago
TinkerHub Sahrdaya successfully conducted an overnight women’s hackathon on February 20 and 21 as part of Tink Her Hack 4.0, an initiative aimed at encouraging and empowering women in technology and innovation. As a continuation of this initiative, the Impact Meet – Women Makers Celebration 2026 was held at Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM), Kochi.
The event brought together venue hosts and representatives from various institutions to celebrate the success and impact of the hackathon. It served as a platform to recognize the efforts of the organizing teams and to connect with members of the startup and innovation ecosystem.
Representatives from TinkerHub Sahrdaya proudly participated in the gathering, engaging with innovators, entrepreneurs, and community leaders. The event highlighted the growing participation of women in technology and entrepreneurship, while fostering collaboration and knowledge sharing within the innovation community.
The program was an inspiring experience for the participants and a proud moment for the institution to be part of an initiative that promotes women’s empowerment in the field of technology and innovation.
Hands-on Robotics Workshop Conducted by CSE Department
5 days ago
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering organized a Hands-on Robotics Workshop on 10 and 11 March 2026 at the CPS Lab, in collaboration with Zlyra Robotics.
The two-day workshop, conducted from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, provided students with practical exposure to the fundamentals of robotics, embedded systems, and automation. Participants gained hands-on experience in assembling and programming robotic systems, helping them understand the real-

world applications of robotics technology.
Inauguration of “Data Pulse” – Data Science Club
5 days ago
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering inaugurated the Data Pulse – Data Science Club on 12 March 2026 at 10:00 AM in the Multimedia Hall, Knowledge Center at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology (Autonomous).
As part of the inauguration, an invited talk titled “Retrieval Augmented Generation: Enhancing LLMs with External Data” was delivered by Prof. Dr. Sabu M K, Professor, Department of Computer Applications, Cochin University of Science and Technology (CUSAT).
100% Pass Achievement in S7 University Examination
1 week ago
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering proudly congratulates the 2022–2026 CSA batch for achieving a 100% pass percentage in the 7th Semester University Examination results.
This remarkable accomplishment reflects the students’ dedication, consistent academic effort, and the guidance provided by the faculty members. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the department and highlights the strong academic performance of the batch.
Alumni Interaction Session Organized by CSE Department
1 week ago
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering organized an Alumni Interaction Session on 11 March 2026 at the Multipurpose Indoor Stadium from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM. The session featured Mr. Melroy Denny, a Software Developer and Assistant Manager (IT) at South Indian Bank, as the guest speaker.
Sahrdaya won the runner-up project award in SRISHTI 2026
1 week ago
Adithya K B, Akshay K Prasad, Alok Shaji and Anjana M S has won the runner-up project award (2nd prize) in the computer science stream for the project titled "Smart Digital Pen for Seamless Handwriting Digitization", guided by Ms Uma E S in SRISHTI 2026

held at Saintgits College of Engineering(Autonomous), Kottayam on 23 & 24 February 2026.
Outstanding Academic Achievement – Perfect 10 SGPA in S7 Examination
1 week ago
Elsa Mariya Anand (S8 CS B) of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology (Autonomous), has secured a perfect 10 out of 10 SGPA in the S7 University Examination. Her remarkable achievement reflects exceptional academic dedication, consistency, and excellence in her studies.
SRISHTI 2026 runner-up project award (2nd prize)
2 weeks ago
Adithya K B, Akshay K Prasad, Alok Shaji and Anjana M S have won the runner-up project award (2nd prize) in the computer science stream for the project titled "Smart Digital Pen for Seamless Handwriting Digitization", guided by Ms Uma E S in SRISHTI 2026 held at Saintgits College of Engineering(Autonomous), Kottayam on 23 & 24 February 2026.
Science & Sustainability – Idea Presentation
2 weeks ago
The Department of Applied Science and Humanities is organizing an Idea Presentation as part of the National Science Day celebration on 02-03-2026. The event, themed “Science and Sustainability,” invites all B.Tech students in sahrdaya to present innovative ideas, concepts, models, or project proposals addressing environmental challenges and sustainable development.
IEEE Sahrdaya SB Announces AGM & Award Day 2026
2 weeks ago
The IEEE Sahrdaya Student Branch is proud to host its Annual General Meeting (AGM) and Award Day.
We are honoured to welcome our Chief Guest, Mr. S. Subramanian, the Founding Architect of the IRCTC and Bombay Stock Exchange systems, as well as a Senior IT Consultant and Project Management Expert.
This special event will celebrate the achievements of our branch and set the stage for the upcoming year.
Please

note that this event is exclusively for EXECOM members.

Google Workshop at Sahrdaya
3 months ago
Happy to share that we successfully hosted the Pre-Hackathon Workshop for the Great Indian Hackathon (GIH) today. The event was organized in association with the India Edu Community by Google for Developers and was partially funded by Google. It was a great success, with students joining us from colleges across Kerala to participate.
The event was effectively coordinated by our students Yash Sanjay Kurup (S6 ECE), Aswin Shaju (S4 ECE), and Abhiramy AS (S4 ECE). This workshop serves as a precursor to the main 24-hour Hackathon, which is scheduled for February 2026.

“Dean – Postgraduate & Doctoral Studies ”
Message from the Dean
As Dean of PG & Doctoral Studies, I strongly believe that project-based research and development is vital for institutional growth. Focusing on real-world, application-oriented projects encourages innovation, strengthens research culture, and bridges the gap between academic learning and societal and industry needs. Such an approach empowers students and researchers to create meaningful, impactful solutions.

Vision
Our Vision
To be a nationally recognized center for quality education and research in diverse areas of computer science engineering, with a strong social commitment.
Our Mission
Impart relevant technical knowledge, skills and attributes along with values and ethics.
Enhance creativity and quality in research through project based learning environment.
Mould Computer Science Engineering Professionals in synchronization with the dynamic industry requirements.
Inculcate essential leadership qualities coupled with commitment to the society.

General Brief
Computer Science & Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
The Department of Computer Science Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology, established in 2002, stands as a beacon of excellence in technical education. Located in Kodakara, Thrissur, Kerala, our department is committed to providing world-class education and fostering innovation in the field of computer science and engineering along with an emphasizing core computing alongside emerging technologies like AI, ML, data science, cybersecurity, and IoT. Modern curriculum covers programming, networks, cloud computing, and software engineering
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.
Industry ready curriculum with Project Based Learning and Skill Development Courses
Cutting-edge research in AI, ML, cybersecurity, and emerging technologies
Strong industry partnerships with excellent placement record

HOD Message

Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Message
From the Head of Department
Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together..e
Dr. Manishankar S
Head of Department
Computer Science Engineering
Qualifications
Ph.D. Computer Science
15+ Years Experiencee
Welcome to Computer Science Engineering
Welcome to the Department of Computer Science Engineering at SAHRDAYA COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING AND TECHNOLOGY. The department of Computer Science Engineering started in 2002 with an intake of 60, the present being 240, and has made astounding progress in the last 15 years. We have strong undergraduate and postgraduate programs in computer science.  The Department was formed with the primary objective of providing world class education in the field of Information Technology, while addressing the problems of today and tomorrow. Right from its inception, the department has been offering excellent infrastructural facilities with a variety of computing platforms to aspiring professional students to meet the burgeoning demands of the IT industry.  The Department with qualified and experienced team of faculty members dedicates to reinforce effective teaching learning process in the ambiance of helpful environment well

supported and stuffed with curricular along with co-curricular activities. Time to time department is organizing student development program and hands-on workshop to overcome curriculum-industry gap. The Department is wide open to innovative ideas, methodologies to establish itself as the most hunted excellent learning center in this part of the state.  Computer Science Engineering students are in high demand as far as industry recruiters are concerned. In recent years, Indian Software Industry has boomed due to rapid increase in globalization consequently required more software engineers. Fundamental goal of our department is to provide innovative and quality education with high standard to achieve academic excellence and provides platform for the students to achieve their career goals.
Dr. Manishankar S
Head of Department, Computer Science Engineering

Program Outcomes

Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes.
PO1
Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems
PO2
Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4)
PO3
Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5)
PO4
Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & i

nterpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8).
PO5
Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6)
PO6
The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7).
PO7
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9)
PO8
Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.
PO9
Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences
PO10
Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one's own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments
PO11
Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

Programmes Offered
Computer Science & Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Programmes Offered
Academic programmes offered by this department
B. Tech – Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning)
Programme Overview
The B. Tech in Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) is a future-focused undergraduate program designed to equip students with strong foundations in computing along with specialized expertise in AI and ML technologies. The program blends core computer science principles with advanced topics such as deep learning, data analytics, natural language processing, computer vision, Generative AI, Agentic AI and intelligent systems. With a strong emphasis on hands-on learning, industry relevance, and innovation, students gain the skills needed to build intelligent applications and solve real-world challenges across domains like healthcare, smart cities, finance, robotics, and automation.
The curriculum integrates theory, laboratory practice, and project-based learning aligned with emerging industry needs. Students are trained using modern tools, frameworks, and cloud platforms, preparing them for careers in AI-driven industries, research, and entrepreneurship
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
4-year full-time

programme, affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
60 seats via KEAM counselling
B.Tech – Computer Science and Engineering
Programme Overview
The B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) is a comprehensive undergraduate program designed to build strong foundations in computing, programming, and modern software systems. The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology was established in 2002 and has over two decades of academic excellence in delivering quality technical education, research, and innovation. The department is supported by experienced faculty, modern laboratories, and a strong culture of industry collaboration and student-driven innovation.
The program emphasizes core computer science principles while offering flexible academic pathways through integrated micro-specializations in Software Engineering, Cyber Security, Cloud and Mobile Application Development, Data Science, and Quantum Computing. This structure allows students to gain deep expertise in emerging domains while maintaining a strong base in fundamental computing concepts.
The curriculum covers essential areas such as algorithms, data structures, operating systems, databases, computer networks, and distributed systems, combined with hands-on labs, industry-oriented projects, and innovation-driven learning. Through the micro-specialization model, students can tailor their academic journey based on career aspirations, enabling them to develop domain-specific competencies aligned with evolving global technology trends. The program blends theoretical rigor with practical exposure, preparing graduates for dynamic careers in industry, research, and entrepreneurship.
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
4-year full-time programme, affiliated

to APJ KTU.Card Text
Annual Intake
240 seats via KEAM counselling, divided across 4 batches (60 seats each).
M.Tech Regular – Computer Science & Engineering
Programme Overview
The M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering offers advanced training in cutting- edge technologies with focus on AI/ML, Data Science, Cyber-Physical Systems, Quantum Computing, and Cybersecurity. The programme equips students with the skills to build intelligent systems, analyze complex data, explore next-generation computational models, and design secure digital ecosystems. Through research-driven learning and industry- aligned coursework, the programme prepares graduates to excel in emerging technology domains
Programme Highlights
Total Duration
2 Years (4 Semesters) affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
06
Ph.D - Computer Science & Engineering
Programme Overview
The Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering is a premier research programme designed to cultivate scholars, innovators, and thought leaders capable of advancing the frontiers of computing and interdisciplinary technologies. With a strong emphasis on original research, the programme enables scholars to explore advanced areas such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Cyber-Physical Systems, Quantum Computing, Cybersecurity, Distributed Systems, Internet of Things, Cloud Computing, and Natural Language Processing The interdisciplinary nature of the programme allows candidates to collaborate across domains including electronics, biomedical engineering, smart infrastructure, and computational sciences, enabling impactful real-world contributions.

Program Educational Objectives (PEO)
Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve
Educational Objectives
On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation.
PEO 1
On successful completion of the Program, the Graduates are expected to: 1. To take up challenging careers in suitable corporate, business or educational sectors across the world, in multi-cultural working environment.
PEO 2
To develop and design innovative and novel solutions to solve real life problems in the domain of computer science.
PEO 3
To be responsible citizens with good team-work skills, competent leadership qualities and holistic values.

Program Specific Outcomes (PSO)
Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme
Specialization Outcomes
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes.
PSO 1
To mould students to understand, analyze and develop computer technologies in the areas such as algorithms, system software, multimedia, web design, big data analytics and networking for efficient design of computer-based systems of varying complexity.
PSO 2
To enhance knowledge in the evolutionary changes in computing and apply standard practices and strategies in software project development using open-ended programming environments in order to deliver a quality product for business success and meet the future challenges.
PSO 3
To enable students to employ modern computer languages, environments, and platforms for creating innovative career paths to be an entrepreneur with social accountability.

Department Advisory Committee
Industry-academia collaboration for curriculum enhancement
We have constituted a Department Advisory Committee (DAC) for the Department of Computer Science to plan the activities for the upcoming semester and to review the department’s performance in the previous semester. The primary objective of the committee is to identify curricular gaps in the Computer Science program and to propose suitable add-on courses, value-added programs, and other academic initiatives to bridge these gaps.  The committee consists of distinguished Alumni, Academicians, Industry Experts from the IT sector, PTWA members, the Head of the Department, the Program Coordinator, and Senior Faculty Members.

Board Of Studies (BOS)
Academic governance and curriculum oversight
The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering is a statutory academic body responsible for designing, reviewing, and continuously updating the curriculum to ensure academic excellence and industry relevance. Comprising senior academicians, industry professionals, and researchers, the BoS ensures that the syllabus reflects current technological advancements, research trends, and professional requirements. It plays a vital role in maintaining academic standards, integrating Outcome-Based Education (OBE) principles, and aligning programs with regulatory guidelines while preparing students to meet global career and higher education opportunities.
