ACES ( ASSOCIATION OF COMPUTER ENGINEERING STUDENTS )
Fostering excellence in technical education and student development
Vision
To be a nationally recognized center for quality education and research in diverse areas of computer science engineering, with a strong social commitment.
Mission
1. Impart relevant technical knowledge, skills and attributes along with values and ethics.
2. Enhance creativity and quality in research through project based learning environment.
3. Mould Computer Science Engineering Professionals in synchronization with the dynamic industry requirements.
4. Inculcate essential leadership qualities coupled with commitment to the society.
Association Objectives
Academic Enrichment
The association aims to promote excellence in computer science education by organizing seminars, workshops, tutorials, coding sessions, and expert lectures for B.Tech and M.Tech students
Research and Innovation Promotion
It encourages research, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration through project exhibitions, hackathons, paper presentations, and research discussion forums.
Industry–Academia Interaction
The association seeks to strengthen c

onnections with industry professionals, startups, and research organizations through guest lectures, internships, industrial visits, and collaborative programs.
Skill Development
It focuses on enhancing technical, analytical, and problem-solving skills in emerging areas such as artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, cloud computing, and software engineering
Student Collaboration and Mentorship
The association fosters collaboration between B.Tech and M.Tech students by creating mentorship opportunities, peer learning platforms, and joint technical activities.
Professional Development
It prepares students for higher studies, research careers, entrepreneurship, and employment through career guidance, competitive examination support, and resume-building initiatives

Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids

Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction

Case Study

One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
Activity – Based Learning
At the end of every topic, students are instructed to design innovative products in their activity book
Download PDF
Hands On - Training
Hands-on training is a practical approach to learning that emphasizes direct experien

ce and
active participation. Rather than relying solely on theoretical instruction, this method allows
learners to engage with tools, equipment, or software in real-world or simulated
environments. By doing the work themselves, participants are better able to understand
concepts, develop critical skills, and retain knowledge more effectively. Hands-on training is
especially valuable in technical and skill-based fields, where learning by doing leads to greater
confidence, competency, and job readiness.
Download PDF
Video Lecturers
YouTube Channel:
Students are provided with video lectures for some courses that they can access while they are
away from the Institute
Video lectures by the faculty members of our department are available on the YouTube
Channel.
Download PDF
Case Study Demonstration
A Case Study Demonstration is a detailed presentation of a real or hypothetical situation to explain concepts, analyze problems, and propose solutions.
Download PDF
Virtual labs
Virtual labs:  To provide remote-access to simulation-based Labs in various disciplines of
Science and Engineering
Download PDF
Roleplay
Roleplay is a technique that allows students to explore realistic situations by interacting with
other people in a managed way to develop experience and trial different strategies in a
supported environment.
Roleplay stimulates the imagination and enhances social development, encourages friendship
through cooperation, listening, and turn-taking. This activity makes the students develop their
knowledge on a specified topic. It solves a particular issue, which creates an opportunity for
decision making in a given environment.
Students are instructed to complete the task by role-playing by interacting with their peers
and try to complete the task assigned to them in their spe

cific role.
Download PDF
Virtual teaching
Google Classroom
Creating a Google classroom using google app. Students are made to join as members of the
Google classroom. Lecture materials, Assignments, Quiz questions are posted in the online
classroom.
Download PDF
Designing innovative products:
At the end of every topic, students are instructed to design innovative products in their activity book.
Download PDF
Digital Submissions
etlab is a centralized and automated administration. It is a non-conventional method that
supports collaborative learning, creating and delivering learning materials, generating reports,
student notifications, etc.
Digital submissions: Posting the teaching materials to facilitate blended learning
Download PDF
Mastery Learning
Project-Based Learning
Project-Based Learning focuses on developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills in
the students. Its inquiry-based method of learning to solve the problems given as projects to
the students is a style of active learning. Team activities and team projects help students to
encounter their ideas to promote self-development and growth.
Download PDF
Classroom Quiz
A Classroom Quiz is a short assessment conducted during or after a lesson to evaluate students’ understanding of the topic taught.
Download PDF
MOOCs
NPTEL
The main objective of the National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning (NPTEL) is
to enhance the quality of engineering and science education in the country by developing
content for undergraduate and postgraduate curricula using video and web-based courses.
These courses cover the syllabi prescribed by universities and approved by AICTE.

Our Foundation
Vision & Mission
Our Vision
To evolve as a center of excellence in electronics and communication engineering that molds technically sound, socially responsible technocrats.
Our Mission
Provide excellent academic and practical environment that nurtures and
transforms students into technically competent professionals with innovative
skills.
To equip the students to meet the growing challenges of the society through
constant interaction with research organizations and industry.
Inculcate professional values, entrepreneurial skills and social commitment
and responsibilities with a passion for holistic excellence.

General Brief
Electronics & Communication Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
Established in 2002, the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology is one of the institution’s oldest and most reputed departments, offering B.Tech and M.Tech programs that integrate core electronics, communication systems, and embedded technologies. With strong academic guidance, modern laboratories, and a practical, industry-oriented learning environment, the department equips students to design innovative electronic and communication solutions and prepares them for careers in telecommunications, embedded systems, automation, IoT, and advanced electronics.
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.
Industry-aligned curriculum with hands-on learning and project-based pedagogy.
Advanced research and innovation in core and emerging ECE domains.
Strong industry engagement ensuring quality training and excellent placement outcomes.

HOD Message
Electronics & Communication Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Message
From the Head of Department
Be part of a culture of excellence and innovation, shaping ideas into impact for the future
Dr. Ambily Francis
Head of Department
Electronics and Communication Engineering
Qualifications
Ph.D. Electronics and Communication Engineering
15+ Years Experience
Welcome to Electronics and Communication Engineering
The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology is one of the pioneering departments of the institution, established in 2002. Over the years, the department has grown into a centre of academic excellence, offering B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering, M.Tech in Embedded Systems, and Ph.D. research programmes, catering to the evolving demands of industry and research.
The department is supported by a team of highly qualified and dedicated faculty members who actively engage in teaching, research, consultancy, and professional development. Our academic environment is strengthened by well-equipped laboratories and advanced research facilities that provide students with hands-on exposure to emerging technologies. Notable among these are the C2S VLSI Laboratory granted under MeitY, Cyber-

Physical Systems (CPS) Laboratory in collaboration with IICT, Germany, and an IoT Laboratory funded by IIT Bombay. These specialized labs enable students to gain practical experience in VLSI design, embedded systems, IoT, and cyber-physical systems.
To bridge academia and industry, the department maintains active Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with reputed organizations including C-DAC, IIT Palakkad, Star Innovations, and Inker Robotics, along with several other industry and research partners. These collaborations facilitate joint research, training programs, internships, and student projects.
Our students have consistently secured prestigious internships and project placements at leading institutions and organizations such as IIT Palakkad, CUSAT, DRDO, NIT Surathkal, C-DAC, Hycon, WACE Electronics, and Inker Robotics. Such opportunities provide valuable exposure to real-world engineering challenges and research culture, enhancing their professional competence.
Innovation and research culture are strongly encouraged in the department. Students actively participate in national-level project competitions, hackathons, and technical events, winning awards and project grants regularly. Industrial training is an integral part of the curriculum, ensuring that graduates are industry-ready and globally competitive.
With strong academic foundations, advanced infrastructure, industry collaborations, and a focus on research and innovation, the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering continues to nurture competent engineers and researchers who contribute meaningfully to technological advancement and nation-building.
Dr. Ambily Francis
Head of Department, Electronics and Communication Engineering

Programmes Offered
Academic programmes offered by this department
B.Tech - Cyber Physical Systems
Programme Overview
B.Tech program in Cyber Physical Systems (CPS) is being offered from the current academic year with a sanctioned intake of 30 students. This autonomous program is designed to provide an interdisciplinary learning experience integrating electronics, computing, control systems, IoT, and artificial intelligence, with a strong focus on hands-on training, industry relevance, and real-world problem solving.
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
4-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
30
B.Tech- Electronics and Communication Engineering
Programme Overview
The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology was established in 2002 and has over 22 years of academic excellence. The department has a sanctioned intake of 60 students for the B.Tech program. It offers a unique blend of strong theoretical foundation, hands-on laboratory training, industry-oriented curriculum, and active research culture, supported by modern laboratories and experienced faculty.
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
4-year fu

ll-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
60
Integrated M.Tech - VLSI
Programme Overview
B.Tech–M.Tech Program in VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) with a sanctioned intake of 60 students. This integrated program is designed to provide a seamless academic pathway combining undergraduate and postgraduate studies, enabling students to earn both B.Tech and M.Tech degrees upon successful completion. The curriculum emphasizes strong fundamentals, advanced VLSI design, semiconductor technologies, system-level integration, and extensive hands-on training using industry-standard EDA tools, preparing graduates for high-impact careers in the semiconductor industry, research, and advanced technology development.
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
5-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
60
M.Tech - Embedded Systems
Programme Overview
M.Tech program in Embedded Systems since 2002. Currently, the program is being conducted as an autonomous batch, with the present cohort being the third autonomous batch. The program is designed to provide advanced knowledge and practical expertise in embedded hardware, software, IoT, and real-time systems, enabling students to excel in research, industry, and innovation-driven careers.
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
2-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
6
M.Tech - VLSI
Programme Overview
Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) is offering a specialized VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) program under the autonomous batch, with a sanctioned intake of 12 students. The program is designed to provide in-depth theoretical knowledge and hands-on expertise in IC design, verification, semiconductor technologies, and EDA tools, preparing students f

or high-end careers in the semiconductor industry, research, and advanced system design.
Programme Highlights
Programme Details
2-year full-time programme, affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
12

Program Outcomes
Electronics & Communication Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes.
PO1
Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO2
Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4)
PO3
Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5)
PO4
Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, mode

lling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8)
PO5
Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6)
PO6
The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7).
PO7
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9)
PO8
Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.
PO9
Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences.
PO10
Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
PO11
Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

Program Educational Objectives (PEO)
Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve
Educational Objectives
On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation.
PEO 1
To demonstrate technical competency and leadership to become professional electronics engineers leading to a successful career.
PEO 2
To adapt to current trends by engaging in lifelong learning.
PEO 3
To demonstrate commitment towards sustainable development for the betterment of society.

Program Specific Outcomes (PSO)
Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme
Specialization Outcomes
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes.
PSO 1
Apply the fundamental knowledge of Electronics and Communication Engineering to design and implement various types of electronic systems in communication, signal processing and embedded systems domain.
PSO 2
Follow and adapt to the dynamic trends in the field of Electronics Engineering.
PSO 3
Apply electronic hardware and software tools to find the solutions for problems encountered by the society.

Department Advisory Committee
Industry-academia collaboration for curriculum enhancement
We have constituted a Department Advisory Committee for planning the upcoming semester and evaluating the department activities in the previous semester. The main objective is to identify the curricular gaps and planning of add-on courses & other activities to fill the curricular gaps. The Committee comprises of Alumni, Academicians, Industrial Experts, PTWA members, HOD, Program Coordinator and Senior Faculty Members.No objective found.

Board Of Studies (BOS)
Academic governance and curriculum oversight
The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Electronics and Communication Engineering Department is constituted to design, review, and update the curriculum and academic framework in alignment with emerging technologies, industry requirements, and regulatory guidelines. The BoS ensures that the program outcomes, teaching–learning processes, and assessment methods maintain high standards of quality to prepare graduates for successful careers in electronics, communication systems, embedded technologies, research, and entrepreneurship.

Department Labs
Electronics & Communication Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
ECE
Department Laboratories
State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology
The ECE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education.
8
Labs
24/7
Wi-Fi
300+
Systems
ECE Lab Facilities
6 / 6
Lab Details
COMMUNICATION  LAB
Description: The Communication Laboratory provides students with practical exposure to the principles and techniques of analog and digital communication systems. The laboratory enables hands-on learning in signal transmission, modulation and demodulation techniques, noise analysis, and system performance evaluation. It emphasizes understanding real-world communication behavior through experimentation, measurement, and analysis using standard laboratory instruments and simulation tools. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge : Ms. Binet Rose Devassy
MICROCONTROLLER  LAB
Description: The Microcontroller Laboratory provides students with practical exposure to embedded system development using microcontroller-based platforms. The laboratory enables hands-on learning in prog

ramming, interfacing, and control of peripherals such as sensors, actuators, and communication modules. It emphasizes developing real-time applications by integrating hardware and software components in a structured learning environment. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge : Ms. Reshma P S
ANALOG  CIRCUITS AND  SIMULATION  LAB
Description: The Analog Circuits and Simulation Laboratory equips students with hands-on experience in the design, analysis, and simulation of analog electronic circuits. The laboratory supports practical learning in amplifier circuits, oscillators, filters, and power supplies, combining theoretical understanding with circuit-level implementation. Emphasis is placed on circuit behavior, performance evaluation, and simulation-based validation. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge :Ms. Siji Joseph
C P S  LAB
Description: The Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) Laboratory provides Electronics and Communication Engineering students with hands-on exposure to the design, integration, and validation of intelligent systems that tightly couple computation, communication, and physical processes. The laboratory supports practical learning in areas such as embedded systems, Internet of Things, sensors and actuators, control systems, and real-time system integration using advanced development platforms and simulation tools. It emphasizes translating theoretical concepts into functional cyber-physical prototypes, system-level implementations, and real-world applications, while enabling experimentation, performance evaluation, and innovation aligned with national CPS and smart technology initiatives. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge :Dr. Jensha Haennah J H
V L S I &  LOGIC  DESIGN  LAB
Description: The VLSI & Logic Design Laboratory offers students a strong practical fo

undation in digital system design and integrated circuit development. The laboratory enables hands-on learning in logic design, digital circuit analysis, and VLSI system modeling using modern simulation and design tools. It focuses on developing design thinking by guiding students from basic logic concepts to complex system-level architectures, while emphasizing correctness, optimization, and verification of digital designs through structured experimentation. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge :Ms. Chinchu Jose
RESEARCH  LAB &  C2 S  LAB
Description: The Research and Chips to Startup (C2S) Laboratory provides Electronics and Communication Engineering students with hands-on exposure to research-driven experimentation and semiconductor system design. The laboratory supports practical learning in areas such as signal processing, embedded systems, communication technologies, and VLSI design, using advanced simulation and industry-standard EDA tools. It emphasizes translating theoretical concepts into functional research models, chip-level design modules, and prototype implementations, while enabling data analysis, system validation, and innovation aligned with national semiconductor and research initiatives. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge :Ms. Vidyamol K
SIGNAL  PROCESSING AND  COMPUTING  LAB
Description: The Signal Processing and Computing Laboratory provides students with practical exposure to the analysis and processing of real-world signals using computational tools and algorithmic approaches. The laboratory supports hands-on learning in areas such as digital signal processing, data analysis, and numerical computing, enabling students to model, analyze, and interpret signals from diverse applications. Emphasis is placed on algorithm development, performance evaluati

on, and visualization to strengthen problem-solving and analytical skills. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge :Dr. Jensha Haennah J H
PROJECT  LAB
Description: The Project Laboratory provides students with a dedicated environment to conceptualize, design, and implement engineering solutions through project-based learning. The laboratory supports individual and team projects across diverse domains, encouraging students to apply theoretical knowledge to real-world problems. It emphasizes system design, integration, testing, documentation, and presentation, while fostering independent thinking, creativity, and research orientation. Systems
Features:
Faculty Incharge :Ms. Anju Babu

Memorandums of Understanding
Strategic partnerships with industry and academic institutions
The department has established formal Memorandums of Understanding with leading industry partners and academic institutions to foster collaborative research, student internships, faculty development, and knowledge exchange.
9
Total MoUs
9
Focus Areas
100%
Active Status
0
Documented
Partnership Agreements
Star Innovations
30/11/2021
Collaboration with Star Innovations Pvt. Ltd. for industrial training, workshops, internships, and placement support.
Focus:
Industrial trainings, technical workshops, guest lectures, internships, and placement activities.
Scope:
Enhancing student skills through hands-on programming workshops, industry-oriented training sessions, expert interactions, and exposure to real-world software development practices.
Benefits:
Industry exposure, practical programming experience, improved employability, internship and placement opportunities, and access to expert-guided advanced technical training.
Jubilee Mission
08/12/2016
Jubilee Mission committed to quality medical education, advanced clinical care, research, and community-orien

ted healthcare services.
Focus:
Focuses on quality medical education, advanced healthcare services, clinical research, and community-oriented healthcare.
Scope:
The collaboration includes training, clinical exposure, research support, knowledge exchange, and academic–industry cooperation in healthcare and allied fields.
Benefits:
Enhances practical learning, clinical skills, research opportunities, healthcare innovation, and overall capacity building.
Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC) India
06/09/2022
Collaboration for advanced computing education, research projects, expert training, and facility development on campus.
Focus:
Expert lectures, R&D collaboration, setting up advanced computing labs, industrial training, pre-placement training, faculty exchange, joint conferences, methodology workshops, and professional development programs.
Scope:
A one-day hands-on training on “VEGA Processors and Ecosystems – the first indigenous development board by CDAC” was conducted on 13-05-2022 for 2020–24 batch students and selected students from the 2019–23 batch.
Benefits:
Provides exposure to indigenous processor technology, enhances student technical competence, supports research collaborations, and boosts placement readiness through CDAC-supported training.
Indian Institute of Technology, PKD(IITPKD)
10/01/2025
Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad is dedicated to excellence in education, advanced research, innovation, skill development, and impactful collaboration with industry and society.
Focus:
Excellence in education, cutting-edge research, innovation, skill development, and meaningful industry and societal collaboration.
Scope:
Collaboration in academic programs, research, innovation, and skill development initiatives. This may involve joint training

programs, faculty development, student engagement activities, curriculum support, workshops, seminars, and knowledge exchange aimed at capacity building and societal impact.
Benefits:
Enhance academic and professional competencies through shared expertise, strengthen faculty and student skill development, promote applied research and innovation, and foster meaningful industry and societal engagement. It will also support capacity building, knowledge exchange, and the creation of sustainable educational and training outcomes.
Additional Skill Acquisition Programme (ASAP)
15/11/2018
Collaboration to deliver ASAP courses and train faculty as master trainers under the Advanced Skill Development Centre division.
Focus:
Running skill development courses, faculty training, and strengthening vocational and industry-oriented programs.
Scope:
Provides structured skill training for students and equips faculty with advanced teaching and training capabilities, expanding institutional capacity in delivering high-quality skill development programs.
Benefits:
Enhanced student employability through certified ASAP courses, improved faculty competency as master trainers, and better access to industry-aligned skill development opportunities.
Agappe Diagnostics LTD
28/11/2018
Agappe Diagnostics Ltd engaged in the development, manufacturing, and distribution of high-quality diagnostic reagents, kits, instruments, and automation solutions.
Focus:
Focuses on innovation, research, and accessible healthcare solutions, supporting clinical laboratories and healthcare institutions in India and globally.
Scope:
The collaboration will focus on training, skill development, research support, industry exposure, and knowledge exchange in medical diagnostics and related technologies.
Benefits:
Enhance sk

ill development, provide industry exposure, strengthen practical learning, support research initiatives, and improve employability through industry–academia engagement.
SCTIMST-TIMED
01/12/2021
Collaboration for training, IPR awareness, research support, and innovation development.
Focus:
Awareness sessions, webinars, training programs, and workshops on IPR and technology transfer.
Scope:
Support for teachers, students, researchers, and startups through access to services, guidance, and innovation-related programs.
Benefits:
Expert guidance on patenting strategy, enhanced research opportunities, exposure to technology transfer, and capacity-building for startups and students.
Part Matters Pvt Ltd

Practical industry exposure, professional development opportunities, and access to laboratory resources when required.
Jobin and Jismi IT Service

ELECSTA
Electronics and Communication Students Technical Association
Vision
To evolve as a Center of Excellence in Electronics and Communication Engineering that moulds technically sound, socially responsible technocrats.
Mission
Provide excellent academic and practical environment that nurtures and
transforms students into technically competent professionals with innovative
skills.
To equip the students to meet the growing challenges of the society through
constant interaction with research organizations and industry.
Inculcate professional values, entrepreneurial skills and social commitment
and responsibilities with a passion for holistic excellence.
Association Objectives
Organize Technical and Professional Activities
Conduct workshops, expert talks, hands-on sessions, and industrial visits to enhance practical exposure and industry readiness.
Promote Awareness of Emerging Technologies and Ethics
Create understanding of new technological trends, ethical practices, and their impact on society.
Encourage Learning beyond the Curriculum
Expose students to current advancements and interdisciplinary developments in engine

ering.
Develop Leadership and Teamwork Skills
Foster communication, collaboration, and leadership through student-driven events and activities.
Foster Innovation and Entrepreneurship
Support idea development, incubation, and mentoring for start-ups and innovative projects.
Provide a platform for Idea Sharing and Collaboration
Enable students to exchange ideas, showcase projects, and share best practices.

Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids
Collaborative Learning
Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction
Alumni Interaction
Case Study
Mastery Learning
One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
Interactive Classroom – Live Coding in Classroom (AI)
The instructor writes and runs AI code live while explaining each step, and students observe the real-time output and logic. This hands-on approach keeps students engaged and h

elps them understand how AI concepts work in practice.
ICT Supported Learning – AI Etlab Quiz
Students use the AI Etlab platform to take online quizzes that assess their understanding of AI concepts. Instant feedback and automated evaluation help improve learning and engagement.
Real-life example – Automatic Streetlight (CAM)
An automatic streetlight uses a light sensor to detect darkness and sends the signal to a controller. The controller processes this data and automatically turns the streetlight ON at night and OFF during daylight, saving energy.
ICT Supported Learning – Design Thinking (NPTEL Assignment)
Students use the NPTEL online platform to learn design thinking concepts through videos and digital resources. Assignments and quizzes help them apply these ideas using ICT tools for effective problem solving.
Collaborative Learning – Group Project: Traffic Light System with Two Modes
Students work in teams to design a traffic light system that operates in normal and emergency modes. Through collaboration, they apply logic, timing control, and problem-solving skills to build and test the system.
Interactive Classroom – Classroom Group Study Project
Students work in small groups to discuss a problem, share ideas, and collaborate on a solution. This approach encourages active participation, teamwork, and deeper understanding of the topic.
Real-life example – Burglar Alarm (CAM)
A burglar alarm uses a sensor to detect motion or a door opening and sends this data to a controller. The controller analyzes the input and, if an intrusion is detected, activates an alarm or sends an alert to the owner.
Efforts to keep students engaged – AWP Animation (Ionosphere)
An animated visualization of the ionosphere helps students see how radio waves travel, reflect, and bend in real

time. This makes the concept more interactive and easier to understand compared to static diagrams.

Our Foundation
Vision & Mission
Our Vision
Evolve as a Centre of Excellence in Electrical and Electronics Engineering to mould technically competent, socially responsible, and sustainability-focused professionals of global standing.
Our Mission
To impart quality technical education in Electrical and Electronics Engineering through expert faculty, robust infrastructure, and skill enhancement programs.
Establish an industry supportive ecosystem to effectively address the evolving needs of the industry.
Encourage graduates to tackle challenges through innovation, sustainable practices, and community engagement, upholding strong ethical values.

General Brief
Electrical & Electronics Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
Electrical & Electronics Engineering is an exciting and dynamic field that deals with the technology of electricity, especially the design and applications of circuitry and equipment for power generation and distribution, machine control and communications. Electrical and Electronics Engineers enjoy a high demand service status in modern manufacturing and industrial processes.   It imparts quality education in multifarious disciplines of Electrical, Electronics, Communication, Control Engineering, etc. along with generation, transmission, and distribution of electrical power and a set of subjects especially focusing on the performance and design of DC, AC, and Special Electric Machines.
The course curriculum combined with practical, produce skillfully competent professionals to face modern technological challenges. The department encompasses a fine blend of renowned as well as young and dynamic faculties. The faculty takes active interest in research and development activities. They regularly organize workshops, training programs, and related activities for the benefit of the EEE students’ community. Sponsored research projects are al

so undertaken by them, contributing to the growth of recent research trends in the field of electrical and electronics engineering. The infrastructure and lab facilities are upgraded from time to time and provide a good practical learning and innovative environment for the students and researchers.
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.
Strong industry partnerships with excellent placement records and median salary of INR 3.20 Lakh.

Our Direction
Vision & Mission

Vision
Our Vision
Evolve as a Centre of Excellence in Electrical and Electronics Engineering to mould technically competent, socially responsible, and sustainability-focused professionals of global standing.
Our Mission
To impart quality technical education in Electrical and Electronics Engineering through expert faculty, robust infrastructure, and skill enhancement programs.
Establish an industry supportive ecosystem to effectively address the evolving needs of the industry.
Encourage graduates to tackle challenges through innovation, sustainable practices, and community engagement, upholding strong ethical values.

HOD Message
Electrical & Electronics Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Message
From the Head of Department
Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together
Dr. Vijikala V
Head of Department
Electrical and Electronics Engineering
Qualifications
Ph.D. Electrical Engineering
20+ Years Experience
Welcome to Electrical and Electronics Engineering
Welcome to the Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering at SAHRDAYA COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING AND TECHNOLOGY.
The Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering focuses on providing strong academic foundations and practical skills in key areas of electrical science and technology. Electrical & Electronics Engineering continues to play an important role in modern industries such as power generation and distribution, automation, electric mobility, and communication systems.
Our curriculum combines theory with hands-on laboratory experience to help students understand and apply concepts in Power Systems, Electrical Machines, Power Electronics, Control Systems, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy. The laboratories and facilities are updated regularly to support effective learning and technical development.
The department has a dedicated team of faculty members who are act

ively involved in teaching, research, and student development. Workshops, training programs, and industry-supported activities are organized to enhance student learning beyond the classroom and to keep pace with technological developments.
We aim to create a supportive academic environment where students can develop technical knowledge, problem-solving skills, and professional values. The department remains committed to helping students build successful careers and contribute positively to the engineering field.
Dr. Vijikala V
Head of Department, Electrical and Electronics Engineering

Program Outcomes
Electrical & Electronics Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes.
PO1
Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO2
Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)
PO3
Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5)
PO4
Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based know

ledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8).
PO5
Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6)
PO6
The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7).
PO7
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9)
PO8
Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.
PO9
Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences
PO10
Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
PO11
Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

PSO 2
Apply software and hardware tools to develop electrical and electronic systems and create passion for research and innovation.
PSO 3
Apply software and hardware tools to develop electrical and electronic systems and create passion for research and innovation

Program Educational Objectives (PEO)
Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve
Educational Objectives
On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation.
PEO 1
To utilize broad knowledge of Electrical and Electronics Engineering as a foundation to design, develop, and implement innovative solutions for real-world challenges.
PEO 2
To equip with the skills to adapt to the technological advancements by engaging in lifelong learning and collaboration with industry and academia.
PEO 3
To evolve as socially responsible engineers with a strong ethical commitment towards environmental sustainability.

EEE
Department Advisory Committee
Industry-academia collaboration for curriculum enhancement
The Department Advisory Committee (DAC) for the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department provides strategic guidance and expert recommendations to strengthen academic programs, infrastructure, and industry collaboration. The committee ensures that the curriculum remains aligned with current and future technological advancements, promotes experiential and outcome-based learning, and supports continuous improvement in teaching, research, and student development. It works to enhance industry–academia partnerships, encourage innovation and skill-building activities, and prepare students to excel in the electrical, electronics, power, automation, and emerging engineering domains.ound.

DQAC
Electrical And Electronics Engineering
Department Quality Assurance Cell
The Department Quality Assurance Cell (DQAC) of the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department is responsible for monitoring, evaluating, and enhancing the overall quality of academic and administrative processes. The cell ensures effective implementation of outcome-based education, promotes continuous improvement in teaching–learning practices, and supports faculty development and research initiatives. It works to maintain consistency in academic standards, strengthen internal evaluation mechanisms, and foster a culture of quality, transparency, and accountability. The DQAC also coordinates feedback collection, analysis, and action planning to ensure student-centric learning and sustained academic excellence.

Board Of Studies (BOS)
Academic governance and curriculum oversight
The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department is responsible for formulating, reviewing, and updating the curriculum to reflect emerging technologies, industry standards, and regulatory guidelines. The committee ensures that teaching–learning processes, assessment methods, and program outcomes meet high academic and professional standards. It focuses on strengthening core engineering competencies, promoting innovation and research, integrating industry-relevant skills, and preparing students for careers in power systems, electronics, renewable energy, automation, embedded systems, and other evolving domains of electrical engineering.

Department Labs
Electrical & Electronics Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
EEE
Department Laboratories
State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology
The EEE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education.
7
Labs
24/7
Wi-Fi
300+
Systems
EEE Lab Facilities
6 / 6
Lab Details
A N A L O G  E L E C T R O N I C S
Description: The laboratory consists of a single room with an area of 1040 sq.ft. The Analog Electronics Lab in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering is well equipped with facilities for the under-graduate courses and research oriented studies. In under-graduate courses, students are divided into small groups so as to give them proper exposure. Laying a strong foundation for young engineers, the laboratory makes the students understand the fundamentals of Analog Electronics. The Major equipment includes regulated dual power supplies, cathode ray oscilloscope (CROs), and function generators. Systems
Features:
S3	EEL203 Analog Electronics Lab
E L E C T R I C A L  W O R K S H O P
Description: The workshop includes familiarization of various eq

uipment, tools and techniques used in electrical laboratory and practice of different basic wiring, lighting circuits. Students are benefited in such a way that they get an idea about household wiring and metering systems and various tools. They are also made aware of various safety precautions, electrical shocks and preventions. Students get an idea of use of tools used by electricians, wiring regulations, types of cables and electric accessories including switches, plug, circuit breakers, fuses etc., symbols for electrical wiring schematics e.g. switches, lamps, sockets etc. Systems
Features:
S1 and S2	24ESL006 Basic Electrical and Electronics Workshop
POWER  ELECTRONICS  LAB
Description: The Power Electronics Lab offers hands-on training in power electronic devices and systems, including component analysis and experiments on converters, inverters, choppers, and motor control. Systems
Features:
S6 POWER ELECTRONICS LAB
EEL334
E L E C T R I C A L  M A C H I N E S  L A B
Description: It captures the essence of hands-on learning and experiments with DC and AC machines, which is the core purpose of the lab. Systems
Features:
S4	EEL202 Electrical Machines Lab I
S5	EEL333 Electrical Machines Lab II
M E A S U R E M E N T S &  I N S T R U M E N T A T I O N  L A B
Description: Provides hands-on training in calibrating energy meters, measuring electrical parameters, and using advanced instruments to apply theoretical knowledge practically. Systems
Features:
S3	EEL201 Circuits and Measurements Lab
S6	EEL332 Power Systems Lab
D I G I T A L  E L E C T R O N I C S  L A B
Description: The laboratory consists of a single room with an area of 1040 sq.ft. The Digital Electronics Lab in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering is well equipped with modern facilities for

the under-graduate courses. Students are divided into small groups so as to give them proper exposure. Laying a strong foundation for young engineers, the laboratory makes the students understand the fundamentals of Digital Electronics. The students will have hands-on experience of Logic gates, Combinational and Sequential circuits. The major equipment include digital IC trainers, microprocessor/microcontroller trainer kits, stepper motor interfacing cards, ADC interfacing modules, matrix keyboard interface modules, LED matrix display module, regulated dual power supplies. Systems
Features:
S4	EEL204 Digital Electronics Lab
A D V A N C E D  E L E C T R I C A L  E N G I N E E R I N G  L A B
Description: The laboratory consists of a single room with an area of 1200 sq.ft. The Advanced Electrical Engineering Lab in the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering is well equipped with modern facilities for the different courses and project oriented studies. Small groups of students are formed in order to provide them with appropriate exposure. Laying a strong foundation for young engineers, the laboratory makes the students understand the fundamentals of Control systems. They are given a strong base in Microprocessor, Microcontroller, and MATLAB keeping the objective of lab in mind. Advanced Electrical Engineering lab also includes popular system analysis software MiPower and Mathworks software MATLAB. Systems
Features:
CAPACITY : 30 SYSTEMS

Memorandums of Understanding
Strategic partnerships with industry and academic institutions
The department has established formal Memorandums of Understanding with leading industry partners and academic institutions to foster collaborative research, student internships, faculty development, and knowledge exchange.
5
Total MoUs
5
Focus Areas
100%
Active Status

Documented
Partnership Agreements
CADD Centre Training Services
27/04/2022
As Asia’s biggest network of CAD training centers, CADD Centre Training Services is the training arm of the 30-year-old CADD Centre Group, head quartered at Chennai, India.   Founded in the year 1988, we have grown today as Asia’s biggest network of skill training provider in Computer Aided Design (CAD), Computer Aided Engineering (CAE), and Project Management. We host a wide array of courses, certifications, placement assistance and career planning tools to help students get the most from their education.  Under the guidance of renowned administrators, directors, and faculty, CADD Centre Training Services recently marked its 30 years of excellence. We have also proudly crossed milestones having over 1000+ franchise ou

tlets across 30+ countries during this time span.
Focus:
The focus of this MoU is to enhance technical competency of engineering students by providing training in professional CAD tools and industry-relevant design software from companies like Autodesk, PTC, ANSYS, Bentley and others. The agreement aims to support practical learning, improve software skills required for modern engineering industries and integrate globally recognized design technologies into academic learning and project work at Sahrdaya engineering programs.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU between Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology and CADCENTER is to collaborate in the Autodesk Academic Adoption Partner (AAP) program through which CADCENTER, being an Authorized Training Centre and Authorized Academic Partner of Autodesk, will provide Autodesk product training and Autodesk certification to students and professionals of the institution. The MoU covers both on-site and off-site training by qualified trainers of CADCENTER, using professional design, CAD and engineering tools that are recognized internationally.
Benefits:
The benefits of this MoU include access to professional Autodesk tools, certified training, globally recognized software certifications, exposure to industrial CAD platforms, and skill development aligned with engineering industry requirements. Students receive training from an authorized Autodesk academic partner, which improves their employability, strengthens design knowledge, and gives added value for project work, internships and future career opportunities in engineering design fields.
Download Agreement
Waves Electronics Private Limited (WEPL)
18/11/2024
Waves Electronics Private Limited (WEPL) was founded in 1972 by Mr. C. P. Philipose and Mr. P. I. Chacko, alumni of Nationa

l Institute of Technology, Warangal, South India. Waves is today the market leader in the manufacture of Industrial Battery Chargers, Marine Control Systems, Navigation Light Indicator Panels, Generator Automation, and Automatic Power Factor Correction Control Panels.  WEPL cater to the leaders in Indian industry and export to countries in Europe, South East Asia, Middle East, and Africa. We produce high quality, performance tested, and fully certified control systems that provide a stable operation over the complete life cycle.
Focus:
The primary focus of this MoU is to build a strong linkage between industry and academia by encouraging collaborative research, innovation, and skill development. It aims to enhance SCET’s academic and research capabilities through active participation in WEPL’s industrial processes, design, and development activities. The MoU focuses on bridging theoretical learning with practical exposure by facilitating industrial training, expert lectures, workshops, and curriculum support. It also concentrates on fostering joint product development, knowledge sharing, and providing real-world experience to students and faculty through sustained interaction with WEPL engineers and facilities.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU encompasses comprehensive collaboration between WEPL and SCET to strengthen industry–academia engagement through joint programs, research activities, and knowledge exchange. It includes sharing of facilities, expertise, and resources to enhance education, research, and industrial exposure. The scope further covers joint product development, simulation and design analysis, faculty training, curriculum development support, student internships, industrial training, workshops, and mentoring programs. A Coordination Cell will also be estab

lished at both institutions to monitor, implement, and manage all activities under this MoU.
Benefits:
This MoU provides significant benefits to both institutions. SCET students gain exposure to real industrial environments, develop practical engineering skills, and receive opportunities for internships, project guidance, and mentoring from experienced WEPL professionals. Faculty members benefit from industry-linked training, consultancy opportunities, and enhanced research collaboration. SCET as an institution gains improved academic quality, stronger research output, and greater industry relevance. WEPL benefits by accessing trained student talent, collaborative research support, and academic expertise that can contribute to product development and innovation. Both organizations benefit through mutual knowledge sharing, enhanced capabilities, and the creation of a long-term, impactful industry–academia partnership.
Download Agreement
Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC)
07/06/2022
Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (CDAC) is the premier R&D organization of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) for carrying out R&D in IT, Electronics and associated areas. Different areas of C-DAC, had originated at different times, many of which came out as a result of identification of opportunities.
Focus:
The main focus of this MoU is to promote collaborative teaching, research and development in modern technological domains, and to encourage knowledge sharing between both institutions. It aims to support skill development, enhance academic quality, provide exposure to advanced technologies and develop technological solutions that contribute to socially productive outcomes.
Scope:
The scope of the MoU between Sahrdaya College of Engi

neering and Technology and C-DAC includes collaboration in advanced technology areas such as Cyber Security, Cyber Law, Cloud Computing, 5G Networks, GIS, Power Electronics, Control Systems, VEGA processors and other emerging fields. It also covers academic cooperation through expert lectures, student projects, industrial training, joint conferences, workshops, faculty exchange, research programmes and other mutually agreed activities.
Benefits:
The benefits of this MoU include opportunities for students to receive industrial training, internships, pre-placement training and expert guidance in emerging technologies. It also enables joint research projects, funding possibilities, faculty development programmes, knowledge sharing and enhanced industry–institute interaction. Overall, the MoU strengthens research culture and improves the employability of students.
Download Agreement
Kerala Electrical & Allied Engineering Co. Ltd. (KEL)
18/11/2024
Established in 1964 in the State of Kerala, India, the Kerala Electrical & Allied Engineering Co. Ltd. (KEL) is a multifaceted company fully owned by the State government. Through it’s four production facilities, located in various districts of the State, this ISO 9001 : 2000 complaint company provides basic engineering services / products besides executing projects of national significance for high profile clients like the various defence establishments.  The company manufactures and markets products like general purpose brushless alternators, brushless alternators for lighting and air-conditioning of rail coaches, medium power and distribution transformers as well as structural steel fabrications.
Focus:
The focus of this MoU is to create a strong and sustainable industry–academia interface by promoting collaborative research, kn

owledge exchange, and skill development between KEL and SCET. The partnership emphasizes the enhancement of research and product development capabilities through joint projects, simulation, design analysis, and consultancy activities. It also focuses on strengthening academic quality by involving industry experts in curriculum development, training programmes, and workshops. Additionally, the MoU prioritizes student growth by providing opportunities for internships, industrial training, mentoring, and exposure to real-world engineering practices. Overall, the focus is on fostering innovation, building technical competency, and enabling meaningful academic–industry integration.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU encompasses a comprehensive range of collaborative academic–industry initiatives aimed at enhancing education, research, and industrial exposure for both KEL and SCET. It includes the joint execution of industry–academic programmes such as research, consultancy, training, and development activities, along with the mutual exchange of facilities, expertise, and technical resources. The partnership also covers joint projects involving simulation, design optimisation, analysis, and product development, supported by faculty collaboration in curriculum development, knowledge sharing, and technical training. Student-focused initiatives—such as internships, industrial training, workshops, and structured mentoring—form a key part of the scope. To ensure smooth implementation and oversight of all activities, a Coordination Cell will be established at both institutions.
Benefits:
The MoU offers significant benefits to both KEL and SCET by enabling shared access to expertise, facilities, and collaborative opportunities. SCET benefits through enhanced academic quality, improved res

earch capabilities, and increased student employability through internships, hands-on training, and mentorship provided by KEL professionals. Faculty members gain through technical training, exposure to industrial practices, and participation in joint research and consultancy projects. KEL benefits from access to academic insights, fresh talent, and innovative solutions emerging from collaborative research and student projects. Both institutions gain through mutual knowledge enhancement, shared intellectual property opportunities, and the establishment of a continuous pipeline of skill development, workshops, and conferences. Overall, the partnership strengthens innovation, productivity, and long-term academic–industry synergy.
Download Agreement
Hykon India Limited
05/07/2024
Hykon, Powering the Nation, an ISO 9001 company, is a pioneering presence in the field of power electronics with varied interests in Medical transcription, Information Technology, Solar Energy and so forth. Mr. Christo George, a committed and dedicated technocrat, set it up in 1991.
Focus:
The focus of the MoU is to practically enhance the technical and professional capabilities of students and faculty by providing expert guidance from Hykon and exposing them to current industry practices. It aims to strengthen the research culture within the institution by promoting collaborative research, consultancy, and technology development initiatives. A key emphasis is on bridging academia and industry by enabling students and faculty to work on real-world problems, participate in industry-based projects, and gain insights from practical industrial environments. The MoU also prioritizes improving student employability through structured internships, extended on-the-job training programs, and the possibilit

y of job placements based on performance and available opportunities. Additionally, the partnership fosters continuous knowledge exchange by organizing workshops, seminars, short-term courses, and conferences in areas of mutual interest, ensuring ongoing learning and professional growth.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU covers a wide range of collaborative activities between Hykon and Sahrdaya, including strengthening industry–academic interaction through joint efforts in academic learning, research, training, and professional development. It extends to shared academic and research activities such as consultancy, product development, and technical projects, along with comprehensive skill development initiatives like internships, on-the-job training, expert lectures, workshops, and other competency-enhancing programs. The partnership also involves mutual access to laboratories, testing facilities, and institutional resources on a preferential basis. To ensure effective execution, the MoU establishes coordination cells and a Network Coordination Committee to monitor and manage all activities. The agreement is valid for three years, with provisions for extension, and includes essential legal elements such as confidentiality, financial responsibilities, indemnity, and termination procedures.
Benefits:
The MoU brings significant benefits to both Sahrdaya College and Hykon India Ltd., creating a mutually enriching partnership. For students and faculty at Sahrdaya, it provides enhanced learning opportunities through exposure to industry-grade projects, hands-on training, and real-world applications, along with access to advanced research and product development environments. It strengthens skill development by offering expert mentoring, practical experience, and opportunities for

upskilling through workshops and seminars led by Hykon professionals. The agreement also improves career prospects by providing structured internships, on-the-job training, and the possibility of recruitment based on performance, while giving students preferential access to Hykon’s facilities for academic work. For Hykon, the MoU supports a robust R&D pipeline by enabling collaboration with academic researchers and student project teams, fostering new product ideas and technological solutions. It also helps identify talented students for potential employment and offers cost-effective access to Sahrdaya’s facilities and academic expertise. Both parties benefit through shared intellectual property, stronger industry–academic ties, enhanced innovation, and efficient sharing of responsibilities and costs related to research, monitoring, and coordination.

ADROIT ELECTRICAL STUDENTS ASSOCIATION (AESON)
The curtains of AESON was raised by  Mr. Muralidharan (Deputy Chief Engineer, KSEB)  on 17th September 2012. The AESON of Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology represents students within the Electrical and Electronics Engineering Department. Our goal is to organize social and professional activities which strengthen and enrich the community within the department, and to help publicize various research activities within the department. We organize social events, host talks by guest speakers and students, and help communicate with the department and the students.
Vision
“To create an environment where creativity, research, and technological advancement thrive, enabling students to become pioneers in electrical and related engineering fields.”
Mission
“To build a collaborative community that nurtures creativity, practical competence, and problem-solving abilities, while providing platforms for hands-on learning, research, and career development.”
Association Objectives
Knowledge Advancement Through EEE Programs
To further the knowledge and awareness in the field of Electrical and E

lectronics Engineering through conferences, workshops, seminars and awareness programs.
Professional Guidance Sessions
Inviting Guest lecturers of industrialists, academicians, management personalities to motivate and guide our members.
Leadership & Professional Development
To develop leadership and initiative, and to inculcate a sense of responsibility amongst the members of the Association
Idea Exchange Forum
To create a forum for the exchange of ideas between students and staff of the department and other departments in the school and provide a conducive atmosphere for social contact between the aforementioned.
Industry Collaboration & Career Exposure
To connect students with industry opportunities and career paths.
Community Engagement & Service
To promote social responsibility through outreach and service activities.

Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids
Collaborative Learning
Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction
Alumni Interaction
Case Study
Mastery Learning
One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
Massive Open Online Courses
Faculty members and students are motivated to take up online courses for their subjects from various reputed platforms including NPTEL and Coursera.
Faculty members and students are motivated to take up online c

ourses for their subjects from various reputed platforms including NPTEL and Coursera.
NPTEL Local Chapter:
Our college is having NPTEL Local Chapter: It is a partnership between the college and NPTEL. Many students and faculty members in the department enrol for courses and get certified after the successful completion of the course.
http://nptel.ac.in/LocalChapter
Faculty: Mr. Adarsh S R, Dr. Vishnu Gopan K (Mentors) Course: HUT200 Professional Ethics (2023-27 batch). Total 27 students out of 30 registered, got the certification with 1 Silver and 9 Elite certificates.
Download PDF
ICT Enabled Teaching Learning
Classrooms in the Department are provided with an LCD projector and a computer with LAN/smart board and high-speed internet access that allows for multimedia presentation. The faculty member can alternate between using either black board and the LCD projector or smart board judiciously allowing for a more efficient lecture delivery.
Some classrooms in the department are provided with smartboards.Smartboard teaching modernizes the classroom by integrating technology to make learning more interactive, collaborative, and enjoyable. When used effectively, it supports diverse learning styles, encourages student participation, and helps bridge the gap between traditional and digital education.
Faculty and students can also access the e-resources from the classroom through high speed wireless internet. The students can further access the server of the digital library from the comfort of their home with the help of the URL link provided in the college website.
Central Library-E-resource
e-books and e-journals can be accessed using the link given below.
EBSCOhost: https://search.ebscohost.com
IEEE Xplore: https://ieeexplore.ieee.org
DELNET: www.delnet.in (http://www.deln

et.in/)
Download PDF
Video Lectures
Video lectures are a modern and innovative teaching–learning method that use audiovisual content to deliver concepts in an engaging and interactive way. They allow students to learn at their own pace, revisit difficult topics, and visualize complex ideas through animations, demonstrations, and real-life examples. Video lectures also support blended and flipped classroom models, making learning more flexible, accessible, and effective.
Download PDF
Virtual Teaching Learning
Virtual teaching–learning refers to the use of digital platforms, online tools, and virtual classrooms to deliver education without the need for physical presence. It enables real-time interaction, multimedia-based learning, and flexible access to lessons from any location. This method supports personalized learning, collaboration through online activities, and continuous access to resources, making education more accessible and technology-driven.
Download PDF
Dissection Based Learning
This method involves taking apart electrical machines and measurement instruments to examine their internal components, understand their construction, and explain their working principles. It is an active, hands-on approach that helps students connect theoretical knowledge with the physical structures and functionality of the equipment.
Download PDF
Assignment Based Teaching Learning
Assignment-Based Teaching-Learning is an approach in education where assignments are used as a primary tool for facilitating learning. In this method, students are given specific tasks or projects to complete, which require them to apply what they have learned, conduct research, solve problems, and engage in critical thinking. This approach allows students to take responsibility for their own learning and

encourages them to deepen their understanding of topics beyond the classroom.
Download PDF
Workshops and Technical Talks
Workshops and technical talks are interactive teaching–learning methods where experts share practical knowledge, emerging technologies, and industry trends with students. Workshops provide hands-on experience, skill development, and real-time problem solving. Technical talks expose learners to advanced concepts and real-world applications through expert insights. Together, they enhance practical understanding, encourage curiosity, and bridge the gap between classroom learning and industry practices.
Download PDF
Activity Based Teaching Learning
It is a student-centered approach where learning is facilitated through hands-on activities, experiments, games, or real-world tasks. This method encourages active participation, problem-solving, and collaborative learning, making education more interactive and engaging.
Download PDF
Collaborative Teaching Learning
Collaborative teaching–learning is an innovative TLM where students work together in groups to solve problems, share ideas, and construct knowledge collectively. This method encourages active participation, communication, teamwork, and critical thinking. By learning from peers and engaging in joint activities, students develop deeper understanding and social skills while teachers guide and facilitate the process rather than simply delivering information.
Download PDF
Low-Stakes Testing
It refers to assessment methods that are designed to have minimal consequences for students grades or overall performance. Low-stakes tests focus on encouraging regular learning, self-assessment, and improvement, without the pressure of significant academic penalties. These tests aim to enhance learning by providing s

tudents with opportunities to demonstrate their understanding in a low-pressure environment.
Online quizzes
Classroom quizzes

Our Foundation
Vision & Mission
Our Vision
To be a centre of excellence to mould technically competent Civil Engineers with High Caliber and ethical values to serve the society.
Our Mission
To provide technical expertise with strong emphasis of professional ethical and social commitment.
To mould competent Civil Engineers to take up challenges in the field of Civil Engineering.
To encourage students for pursuing higher education and research.
To undertake professional consultancy services in diverse areas of Civil Engineering

General Brief
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
Established in 2012, Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology's Department of Civil Engineering is a dynamic hub shaping future-ready engineers through rigorous academics, cutting-edge labs, industry projects, and advanced software training. As the "mother of all engineering disciplines," Civil Engineering drives nation-building—from sustainable infrastructure and smart cities to tackling climate change, disaster resilience, and water management. Our graduates launch thriving careers worldwide as Structural, Geotechnical, Environmental, or Transportation Engineers, Project Managers, Urban Planners, and more, backed by a strong alumni network, internships, and faculty mentorship. Join us to blend creativity with engineering, build impactful careers, and transform society.
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.
Hands on learning with real world projects.
MoUs with Core Industries and government Organizations.

To be a centre of excellence to mould technically competent Civil Engineers with High Caliber and ethical values to serve the society.
Our Mission
To provide technical expertise with strong emphasis of professional ethical and social commitment.
To mould competent Civil Engineers to take up challenges in the field of Civil Engineering.

To undertake professional consultancy services in diverse areas of Civil Engineering

HOD Message
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Message
From the Head of Department
Dr Drisya M
Head of Department
CE Department
Qualifications
Ph.D. Civil Engineering
Welcome to CE Department
The Department of Civil Engineering is one of the youngest Departments in Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology. From its inception in 2012, it is striving to excel in different spheres in order to help the students to excel both in curriculum and research.
The department has a core group of highly motivated faculty members having industrial and teaching experience. The knowledge and ideas they have assimilated in various Civil Engineering disciplines are furnished to the students through the best academic practices. The department’s academic and research activities are enhanced by the extensive knowledge of faculty members with advanced degrees and trainings from prestigious institutions.
Our alumni network includes notable figures who hold prominent positions in private and government sectors worldwide. Our department became one of the top choices among students due to increased interactions with academia and industry. We collaborate closely with a variety of government and corporate agencies to guarantee that our research and teaching efforts have a significant societal impact.
Dr Drisya M
Head of Department,

CE Department
