Programmes Offered
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Programmes Offered
Academic programmes offered by this department
B.Tech Lateral Entry– Civil Engineering
Programme Overview
B.Tech lateral entry in civil engineering allows diploma holders to join directly in the second year, shortening the standard four-year program to three years.
Programme Highlights
Intake
3 Nos
B.Tech in Civil Engineering
Programme Overview
Department has established in the year 2012 to equip students with the knowledge and skills required to plan, design, construct, and maintain infrastructure such as buildings, roads, bridges, and water systems. It provides a strong foundation in structural engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation, environmental, and construction management.
Programme Highlights
Programme Duration
4-year full-time programme, Affiliated to APJ KTU.
Annual Intake
120 seats via KEAM counselling, divided across 4 batches (30 seats each).
Career Outcomes
Civil Engineer/ Structural Designer. Tremendous Opportunities in Government and Non-Government Organisations.
M.Tech in Structural Engineering & Construction Management
Programme Overview
The M.Tech in Structural Engineering & Construction Management aims to take up advanced technical and managerial roles in the construction and infrastructure sector. The programme int

egrates structural analysis and design with modern construction management practices, emphasising lab-integrated learning, interdisciplinary flexibility, and strong project
and research orientation.
Programme Highlights
Key Features
Lab-Integrated Learning with advanced software and experimental facilities in structural engineering and construction management.
Project  Research Emphasis through dissertations, industry-oriented projects, and seminars on recent developments.
Intake
6 Nos

Program Outcomes
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes.
PO1
Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO2
Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4)
PO3
Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5)
PO4
Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusio

ns. (WK8).
PO5
Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6)
PO6
The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5, and WK7).
PO7
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws. (WK9)
PO8
Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.
PO9
Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences
PO10
Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
PO11
Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

Program Educational Objectives
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Educational Objectives (PEO)
Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve
Educational Objectives
On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation.
PEO 1
Apply the acquired knowledge and skills  in Civil Engineering to become successful and competent professions, capable of contributing effectively to the domain of civil Engineering and to cater to the needs of the society.
PEO 2
Demonstrate leadership qualities in the execution and management of Civil Engineering projects, upholding professional ethics and sustainability concepts.
PEO 3
Engage in lifelong learning with a quest to keep abreast with the latest developments in Civil Engineering practice.

CE
Program Specific Outcomes
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Specific Outcomes (PSO)
Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme
Specialization Outcomes
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes.
PSO 1
Apply acquired knowledge in technical areas to Civil Engineering problems, including but not limited to Structural, Geotechnical, Transportation, Geo-informatics, Environmental, and Water Resources Engineering.
PSO 2
Design a system, component, or process to meet desired needs within realistic constraints such as economic, environmental, social, political, ethical, health and safety, manufacturability, and sustainability.
PSO 3
Familiarise with various facets of construction and its planning like project scheduling, resource and material management, understand the impact of engineering solutions in a global, economic, environmental, and societal contexts.

Department Advisory Committee
Industry-academia collaboration for curriculum enhancement
We have constituted a Department Advisory Committee for planning the upcoming semester and evaluating the department activities in the previous semester. The main objective is to identify the curricular gaps and planning of add-on courses & other activities to fill the curricular gaps. The Committee comprises of an Alumni, Academician, Industrial Expert, Entrepreneur, PTWA member, HOD, Program Coordinator and Senior Faculty Membersjective found.

Home
Departments
CE
Department Labs
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
CE
Department Laboratories
State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology
The CE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education.
8
Labs
24/7
Wi-Fi
300+
Systems
CE Lab Facilities
6 / 6
Lab Details
E N V I R O N M E N T A L  E N G I N E E R I N G  L A B O R A T O R Y
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
The environmental engineering laboratory offers students hands-on exposure to EPA approved water and wastewater quality testing procedures as is normally done at a water and wastewater treatment plant
M A T E R I A L S  T E S T I N G  L A B O R A T O R Y
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
A Materials Testing (MT) Lab in civil engineering is a facility where the properties and quality of construction materials like cement concrete steel bricks and aggregates are evaluated under controlled conditions. It supports safe and economical structural design by ensuring that materials meet relevant standards and specifications.
G E O T E C H N I C A L  E N G I N E E R I N G  L A B O R A T O R Y
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
A Geotechnical Engineering Laborat

ory is a specialised facility where the engineering behaviour of soil is studied through standard index and engineering tests. It supports the analysis and design of foundations earth structures and soil‑related problems in civil engineering.
S U R V E Y I N G  L A B O R A T O R Y
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
A Surveying Laboratory is a practical facility where students learn how to measure distances angles and elevations for planning and design of civil engineering projects such as roads buildings and irrigation works. It links theoretical surveying principles with hands‑on field practice using both conventional and modern instruments.
C A D  L A B O R A T A R Y
Description: 45 Systems
Features:
A Civil Engineering CAD Lab is a specialised computing facility where students learn digital drafting modelling and basic analysis for civil engineering projects. It supports the transition from manual drawing to modern software‑based design workflows used in professional practice.
C E      W O R K S H O P
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
The primary purpose of the CE Workshop Lab is to familiarise students with common construction materials and basic site operations used in building and infrastructure works. It develops practical skills and safety awareness and an understanding of labour and workmanship requirements that are essential for effective supervision and execution on construction sites.
T R A N S P O R T A T I O N  E N G I N E E R I N G  L A B O R A T O R Y
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
A Transportation Engineering (TE) Lab is a specialised facility where students study highway and pavement materials as well as basic traffic characteristics
through experiments. It links transportation theory with practical testing and design used in road  projects.
F L U I

D  M E C H A N I C S  L A B
Description: 30 Systems
Features:
Fluid Mechanics Laboratory (FM Lab) is a core facility where students experimentally study the behaviour of fluids at rest and in motion and verify fundamental fluid mechanics principles used in civil engineering.

Department Association
Civil Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
PACE
The Civil Engineering Association (CEA) is a dynamic student body formed under the Department of Civil Engineering with the aim of enhancing academic learning through professional interaction, technical exposure, and community engagement. The association acts as a bridge between classroom education and real-world engineering practices, nurturing leadership, teamwork, and innovation among students.
Vision
To nurture competent, innovative, and socially responsible civil engineers by fostering technical excellence, ethical values, and sustainable development through active student engagement and professional collaboration.
Mission
Promote Technical Excellence
Encourage Innovation and Research
Inculcate Social and Environmental Responsibility:
Develop Leadership and Teamwork
Association Objectives
To promote interest and awareness in emerging areas of Civil Engineering
This objective aims to keep students updated with the latest trends, technologies, and research developments in Civil Engineering.
To organize seminars, workshops, expert talks, and industrial visits that complement academic learning
provides opportunities for students to interact with professionals, academicians, and industry experts.
To en

courage students to participate in technical events, model-making competitions, and paper presentations
This objective focuses on enhancing creativity, innovation, and communication skills among students
To foster collaboration among students, faculty, and industry professionals for skill development and career growth
The association acts as a bridge connecting students, faculty members, and the industry.
To cultivate social responsibility by engaging in activities related to sustainable development and community service
encourages students to participate in social outreach programs such as awareness drives on water conservation, waste management, and eco-friendly construction
To enhance leadership, teamwork, and organizational skills among students
provides a platform for students to plan, coordinate, and execute events and activities, helping them develop essential soft skills.

Innovative TLM

Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids
Collaborative Learning
Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction
Alumni Interaction
Case Study
Mastery Learning
One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
(MOOC) NPTEL Coursera
Major core courses in the curriculum are linked with massive open online courses
Faculty members and students are motivated to take online courses from various eminent platforms like NPTEL and Coursera.NPTEL:The main objective of the National Programme on Technology Enhanced

Learning (NPTEL) is to enhance the quality of engineering and science education in the country by developing content for undergraduate and postgraduate curricula using video and web-based courses. These courses cover the syllabi prescribed by universities and approved by AICTE.NPTEL Local Chapter:Our college is having NPTEL Local Chapter: It is a partnership between the college and NPTEL. Many students and faculty members in the department enroll for courses and get certified after successfully completing the course.http://nptel.ac.in/LocalChapterCoursera:Many faculty members and students in the department have completed online courses based on their area of interest. These courses also help the students to advance their skills for career development.https://www.coursera.org/
Download PDF
ACTIVITY BASED LEARNING
Study group activity - Students are instructed to conduct different engaging activities during study group activities. This inculcates leadership, communication skills, presentation skills, and responsibility in students. Context: Learn from other people’s experiences and background knowledge. Gain perspective and point of view, which increases listening and interpersonal skill.
Download PDF
Field and site‑integrated TLM
On‑site teaching sessions at nearby construction sites, bridges, WTPs, or highways with guided worksheets: identify elements, sketch load paths, list materials, and relate to IS codes studied in class
Download PDF
TECHNICAL QUIZES (ONLINE AND OFFLINE)
They convert a one‑way lecture into active learning by forcing every student to think and respond within a short time window.
Concept check quizzes: 2–5 MCQs or numerical concept questions at the start or end of class on the day’s topic (e.g., choice of foundation type, critical flow, degree of in

determinacy).
Application‑based scenario quizzes: short design or field scenarios (e.g., select suitable retaining wall, propose a traffic control measure) where students pick the most appropriate option and justify in one line.
Peer‑instruction quizzes: students first answer individually (using cards or Google Forms), then discuss with neighbours and re‑answer; the shift in responses shows learning gain and stimulates civil‑engineering dialogue in class
Download PDF
ICT, software and simulation TLM
Simulation‑based learning using open or campus‑licensed tools:
STAAD for comparative analysis of different structural systems.
AUTODESK Software for Sketching and 3D modelling.

BME
General Brief
Biomedical Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
Established in 2002, the Department of Biomedical Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology is one of the earliest and most distinguished institution in Kerala. The department offers both M.Tech and B.Tech programs in Biomedical Engineering, blending engineering, medicine, and life sciences to develop professionals capable of designing, testing, and managing advanced medical technologies that improve healthcare delivery.
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.
Accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA), the department is recognized for its academic rigor, outcome-based curriculum, and strong alignment with industry and regulatory standards. Our graduates are highly sought after for their interdisciplinary knowledge and hands-on expertise, shaped by collaborations with leading MedTech industries and exposure to global best practices.
The department has been identified as an Emerging national Center of excellence, conducting flagship training programs on behalf of the Government of India (BIRAC-NBM) and the Government of Kerala (K-DISC, KMTC). Through these initiatives, the department contributes to strengthening Indi

a’s medical device standards, testing ecosystem, and regulatory capacity building.
The department hosts three Center of Excellence that anchor its teaching, research, and innovation ecosystem: GE-Sahrdaya Centre of Excellence for Healthcare Technologies – advancing healthcare innovation and technology transfer.  Sahrdaya-Agappe Innovation Hub – promoting industry-integrated learning and IVD innovation.  Centre for Medical Equipment Testing and Consultancy (CMET) – supporting device safety, performance evaluation, and ISO 13485-based quality system training. These centers serve as national reference points for medical standards, device testing, and in- vitro diagnostics, bridging academia, industry, and regulatory stakeholders.

HOD Message
Biomedical Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Message
From the Head of Department
Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together
Dr.Jis Paul
Head of Department
BME Department
Qualifications
Ph.D. Biomedical Engineering
20+Yrs Experience
Product Design, Medical Devices and Standards, Human Factors Design, Medical Imaging
Welcome to BME Department
The Department of Biomedical Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology, established in 2002, has been a pioneer in offering the B.Tech programme in Biomedical Engineering, one among the very few in the state. The programme, truly interdisciplinary and innovation-driven, continues to evolve with advancements in healthcare technology, emerging as a nationally recognized hub for producing industry-ready biomedical engineers.
Over the years, the department has established itself as a centre for funded research, consultancy, and industrial collaborations. The GE Healthcare Center of Excellence and the Innovation Hub by Agappe Diagnostics stand as milestones in our journey toward bridging academia and healthcare industries. In addition, BIRAC ISO 13485:2016 certified training programmes are being conducted at Sahrdaya as part of the BIRAC-funded project, aimed at strengthening med

ical device innovation, quality systems, and regulatory compliance.
Our students and faculty have consistently brought laurels through their innovations—winning national and international recognitions such as the IEEE Global Humanitarian Technology Award, Gandhian Young Technological Innovation Award, and even securing a U.S. patent. The department also successfully introduced “LUMOS”, a multipurpose decontamination device, contributing significantly during the COVID-19 pandemic.
As we move into 2025 and beyond, the department is committed to advancing research, translational innovation, and global collaborations. We are expanding our partnerships with leading healthcare industries, strengthening our alumni network, and continuously updating our curriculum to align with the latest trends in AI in healthcare, biomedical data analytics, medical imaging, and medical device innovation.
We warmly welcome aspiring engineers who wish to be part of the next generation of healthcare technologists. At Sahrdaya, you will gain the knowledge, skills, and values to innovate for a healthier tomorrow.
Dr.Jis Paul
Head of Department, BME Department

Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 8 program outcomes.
PO1
Engineering Knowledge: Apply mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals to solve complex problems.
PO2
Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, research, and analyze engineering issues to arrive at substantiated conclusions.
PO3
Design/ Development of Solutions: Design systems/components/processes meeting specifications with public health,safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.
PO4
Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research methods (e.g., experiments, analysis) to derive valid conclusions.
PO5
Engineering Tool Usage:  Select and apply modern engineering and IT tools to complex engineering tasks, understanding their limitations.
PO6
The Engineer and The World:  Apply reasoning with contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues in engineering practice
PO7
Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary t

eams.
PO8
Life Long Learning: Recognize the need for ongoing learning and possess the skills to sustain it in a changing technological world.

Programmes Offered
Biomedical Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Programmes Offered
Academic programmes offered by this department
B.Tech Biomedical Engineering
Programme Overview
Established in 2002, the B.Tech program in Biomedical Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology is one of the earliest and most distinguished undergraduate biomedical programs in Kerala. The program was launched with a vision to integrate engineering principles with medical and life sciences to develop competent professionals capable of advancing healthcare technology.
Affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University (KTU) and approved by All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), the program is accredited by National Board of Accreditation (NBA), ensuring adherence to outcome-based education and global quality standards.
The B.Tech curriculum emphasizes:
Strong foundations in engineering, medical sciences, and electronics
Hands-on laboratory training and industry exposure
Medical device design and development
Regulatory standards and quality systems
Internship and hospital-based clinical training
Through collaborations with leading MedTech industries and government-supported initiatives such as BIRAC-NBM, K-DISC, and KMTC, students gain exposure to real-world challenges in medical device innovation, testing

, and compliance.
The department’s Centers of Excellence—including the GE-Sahrdaya Centre of Excellence, Sahrdaya-Agappe Innovation Hub, and CMET—provide students with opportunities in device testing, ISO 13485 quality systems, and in-vitro diagnostics innovation.
Over the years, the B.Tech program has produced graduates who are highly sought after in medical device industries, healthcare technology companies, regulatory bodies, research institutions, and higher education worldwide.
Programme Highlights
Programme  Duration
4-year full-time programme , Affiliated to APJAKTU
Sanctioned Intake
60
M.Tech Biomedical Engineering
Programme Overview
Launched in 2025, the M.Tech program in Biomedical Engineering is designed to build advanced expertise in healthcare technology innovation, regulatory science, and translational research. The program responds to the growing national demand for highly skilled professionals in medical device development, testing, quality assurance, and healthcare technology management.
Affiliated to APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University and approved by All India Council for Technical Education, the M.Tech program leverages the department’s recognition as an Emerging National Centre of Excellence.
Programme Highlights
Sanctioned Intake
6
PhD in Biomedical Engineering
Programme Overview
The Department of Biomedical Engineering proposes to initiate a Ph.D. Programme in Biomedical Engineering to advance high-impact research in healthcare technologies, medical device innovation, and translational biomedical sciences.
Building upon its strong academic foundation since 2002 and the launch of the M.Tech programme in 2025, the department is well-positioned to foster doctoral-level research aligned with national healthcare priorities and global technological

advancements.
The Ph.D. programme will initially be supported by two recognized research supervisors with expertise in interdisciplinary domains of biomedical engineering, enabling focused and high-quality research guidance.

Program Educational Objectives
Biomedical Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Educational Objectives (PEO)
Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve
Educational Objectives
On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 4 educational objectives within a few years of graduation.
PEO 1
Establish the graduates as creative professionals in design, manufacturing, research and testing of biomedical devices ensuring quality and patient-safety.
PEO 2
Pursue advanced courses in biomedical engineering at par with evolving state-of-the-art technologies.
PEO 3
Evolve as successful engineers, entrepreneurs and inspirational leaders in society with moral ethical values.
PEO 4
Engage in continuous learning through professional development and collaborations to nurture future generations of biomedical professionals

Department Advisory Committee
Industry-academia collaboration for curriculum enhancement
We have constituted a Department Advisory Committee for planning the upcoming semester and evaluating the department activities in the previous semester. The main objective is to identify the curricular gaps and planning of add-on courses & other activities to fill the curricular gaps. The Committee comprises of Alumni, Academicians, Industrial Experts, PTWA members, HOD, Program Coordinator and Senior Faculty Members.

Board Of Studies (BOS)
Academic governance and curriculum oversight
The Board of Studies (BoS) of the Biomedical Engineering Department is constituted to design, review and update the curriculum and academic framework in line with emerging technologies, industry requirements, and regulatory guidelines. The BoS ensures that the program outcomes, teaching–learning processes, and assessment methods are of the highest quality to prepare graduates for successful careers in healthcare, medical device development, research, and entrepreneurship.

Department Labs
Biomedical Engineering
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
BME
Department Laboratories
State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology
The BME department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education.
6
Labs
24/7
Wi-Fi
300+
Systems
BME Lab Facilities
6 / 6
Lab Details
ANALOG  ELECTRONIC  LAB
Description: The Analog Electronics Laboratory in the third semester equips Biomedical Engineering students with the knowledge and hands-on skills to design, analyze and test fundamental analog circuits used in medical devices and instrumentation. This lab bridges theoretical concepts with real-world applications in healthcare technology. Systems
Features:
Circuit design for low-noise
precision measurement systems.
ELECTRONICS  WORKSHOP  LAB
Description: The Electronics Workshop Laboratory introduces first-semester students to the fundamentals of electronic components, tools, and practical skills essential for engineering practice. This hands-on laboratory helps students identify, test and use basic electronic components such as resistors, capacitors, diodes, transistors, LEDs and integrated circuits. Students

learn the safe handling of instruments, soldering and desoldering techniques, wiring practices, breadboard prototyping, and basic circuit assembly. Systems
Features:
Through carefully designed exercises
the lab cultivates:  Familiarity with measurement instruments such as multimeters
power supplies and oscilloscopes.  Understanding of circuit diagrams and their physical implementation.  Skills in fault diagnosis and basic repair of simple circuits.  Teamwork
safety awareness and documentation practices.
MEDICAL  EMBEDDED  COMPUTING AND  VIRTUAL  INSTRUMENTATION  LAB
Description: The Medical Embedded Computing and Virtual Instrumentation Laboratory in the fifth semester provides Biomedical Engineering students with hands-on experience in MATLAB programming and virtual instrumentation tools used for modeling, analysis, and visualization of biomedical signals and systems. The laboratory emphasizes software-based implementation of medical data analysis and graphical interfaces Systems
Features:
Translating theoretical biomedical data processing concepts into working MATLAB/VI modules.
CLINICAL  INSTRUMENTATION  LAB
Description: The Clinical Instrumentation Laboratory (BML333) is designed to familiarize students with the instrumentation, operation, and practical aspects of biomedical equipment such as ECG machines, patient monitors, electrosurgical units, colorimeters, ventilators, and infusion pumps. This lab provides students with hands-on exposure to designing, implementing, and troubleshooting electronic circuits used in medical devices, as well as testing and calibration procedures essential for clinical applications. By working directly with diagnostic, therapeutic, and monitoring instruments, students gain valuable practical knowledge that bridges theory with real-wor

ld biomedical engineering practices.  Systems
Features:
Bridging theory with real-world applications in biomedical engineering.
BIOMEDICAL  SIGNAL &  IMAGE  PROCESSING  LAB
Description: The Biomedical Signal and Image Processing Laboratory in the seventh semester equips students with advanced skills in the acquisition, analysis, and interpretation of biomedical signals and medical images. This lab integrates concepts from digital signal processing, image analysis, and machine learning to solve real-world healthcare challenges. Systems
Features:
Developing algorithms for early diagnosis
monitoring
and decision support.
DIGITAL  ELECTRONICS  LAB
Description: The Digital Electronics Laboratory in the third semester introduces Biomedical Engineering students to the fundamentals of digital logic, circuit design, and practical implementation techniques used in modern biomedical instruments. This lab enables students to build, test, and troubleshoot digital circuits that form the core of medical devices and embedded healthcare systems. Systems
Features:
Translating digital logic theory into functional hardware.

TMA-HYKON Innovation Challenge 2025–26
Aleena Paula, Amal Krishna, Ashin Jiboo, and Abhikayma participated in the TMA-HYKON Innovation Challenge 2025–26 with their project titled “Ionized Calcium Detector from Whole Blood for Hypocalcemia Mitigation in Livestock” and secured the Third Prize in the competition.

3rd Prize – Smart Leap Project (Srishti Project Expo – Tharang Tech Fest)

YIP District Level
The project team “Po

int of Care BV Detection Device using Electrochemical Lactate Sensor & pH Strips”, comprising Anjaly Joseph, Joel Varghese Shibu, Sneha P. S., and Bastin Antony, achieved a remarkable milestone by being selected for the Young Innovators Programme (YIP) at the College Level (CAT 2_4) and advancing to the District-Level round. Their solution presents a low-cost, rapid diagnostic platform integrating electrochemical lactate sensing with pH-based biochemical screening—an innovative approach aimed at enabling early and accessible detection of Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) in point-of-care settings. This recognition highlights the team's strong problem-solving ability and their commitment to developing impactful healthcare technologies.
08/10/2025

Arts 2K26
EBSA

2025-26
TIH-IoT, IIT Bombay -Grant-(NM-ICPS).
Mr. Jibin Jose and Ms. Supriya Mary Sunil from the Biomedical Department have secured a major grant of ₹49.78 lakh from TIH-IoT, IIT Bombay, under the Government of India’s National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS). Their project, titled “Breast Cancer Screening Device using Transillumination and Near Infrared Spectroscopy,” focuses on developing a cost-effective, non-invasive screening technology aimed at enabling early detection of breast cancer. Supported under the Technology Development Project (TDP) scheme, this initiative underscores the team’s commitment to advancing accessible and impactful healthcare innovations.

IPTIF–AGNI UG Fellowship 2025–26
Three projects from the Biomedical Department have secured positions among the four shortlisted proposals for the prestigious IPTIF–AGNI UG Fellowship 2025–26, recognized for their societal relevance, innovation, and translational potential. The selected projects are:  “Foot Drop Rehabilitation System using Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES)” guided by Dr. Reshma Jose.  “Suction Device for Emergency Relief of Airway Obstruction” guided by Ms. Supriya Maty Sunil.  “Development of Disposable Electrochemical Sensor for First-Line Tuberculosis Drug – Isoniazid” guided by Dr. Dhanya Gangadharan (Biotechnology) and Dr. Jeethu Raveendran (Biomedical).  This strong presence showcases the department’s leadership in healthcare innovation and its commitment to translating ideas into meaningful societal impact.

Memorandums of Understanding
Strategic partnerships with industry and academic institutions
The department has established formal Memorandums of Understanding with leading industry partners and academic institutions to foster collaborative research, student internships, faculty development, and knowledge exchange.
5
Total MoUs
5
Focus Areas
100%

0
Documented
Partnership Agreements
Kerala Veterinary and Animal Science University
29/09/2025
This Memorandum of Understanding establishes a collaborative framework between the two institutions to promote cooperation in research, academics, product development, validation, entrepreneurship, and innovation. The MoU aims to facilitate the sharing of knowledge, technical expertise, and resources to enhance mutual learning and development in science, engineering, medical, veterinary, and allied disciplines. Through joint programs, training opportunities, exchange of data, and organized academic events, both parties will work together to create impactful research outcomes, strengthen educational processes, and support innovation-driven activities.
Focus:
The primary focus of this MoU is to encourage collaboration in research and developmen

t, student innovation, and interdisciplinary knowledge sharing. It aims to support joint academic initiatives, exchange of expertise in engineering, medical, and veterinary technologies, and engagement in product development and validation. The MoU also focuses on fostering entrepreneurship, facilitating professional training programs, and supporting innovation ecosystems through cooperation with Technology Business Incubators and startup activities.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU includes joint research activities, exchange of publications and data, and collaborative development programs across engineering, biotechnology, veterinary science, farm mechanization, and healthcare technologies. It also extends to supporting student project works, organizing seminars, workshops, conferences, and short-term programs of mutual interest. Additionally, the MoU covers training opportunities, shared access to expertise and facilities, participation in MOOC-based learning, and coordinated arrangements for stakeholder engagement, including startups and innovation centers. Specific collaborative projects will be defined under separate agreements as required.
Benefits:
This MoU provides extensive benefits for students, faculty, and research communities of both institutions. It enhances access to specialized technical expertise, research facilities, training programs, and domain knowledge across engineering, medical, and veterinary technologies. Students gain hands-on exposure to real-world projects, innovation practices, and entrepreneurship development opportunities. Faculty members receive platforms for collaborative research, joint publications, and professional networking. The partnership also strengthens institutional capability by enabling shared resources, promoting interdiscipli

nary research, supporting startup ecosystems, and creating pathways for product development, validation, and funded innovation projects.
Jubilee Mission Medical College and Research Institute
08/12/2016
This Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) establishes a collaborative framework between the two institutions to foster academic, research, and innovation-driven activities. The scope of the MoU includes promoting joint research, sharing expertise, facilitating skill development, and enabling the use of laboratories and testing facilities across both campuses. It also supports student training, project opportunities, and knowledge exchange for mutual growth.
Focus:
Academic collaboration, research promotion, innovation, and healthcare solution development.
Scope:
Student skill enhancement through shared laboratories, joint research, knowledge exchange, and hospital-based training.
Benefits:
Industry and hospital exposure, access to advanced testing facilities, improved research opportunities, and mutual academic enrichment.

Dr. Rani Menon MaxVision Eye Care
03/03/2025
This Memorandum of Understanding establishes a collaborative partnership between Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology and Dr. Rani Menon MaxVision Eye Care to advance academic research, technological innovation, and training in ophthalmology and biomedical engineering. The agreement provides a structured framework for joint research, internships, clinical exposure, medical device development, and community health initiatives. Through this collaboration, both institutions aim to strengthen their expertise, enhance student training, and promote advancements in ophthalmic techn

ologies and healthcare practices for mutual benefit.
Focus:
The focus of this MoU is to promote research-driven innovation in ophthalmic technologies, foster academic collaboration, and create opportunities for experiential learning in biomedical and clinical environments. It emphasizes joint research publications, patent development, hands-on exposure to ophthalmic diagnostic tools, and skill enhancement for both students and faculty. The collaboration also focuses on strengthening clinical knowledge, patient care understanding, regulatory compliance, and medical device validation processes.
Scope:
The scope of this collaboration includes establishing a research framework for ophthalmic technology development, undertaking joint research projects, and presenting research outcomes at national and international platforms. It also includes filing joint patents and supporting innovation in medical devices. The MoU covers structured training programs, internships, clinical workflow learning, exposure to ophthalmic diagnostic techniques, and faculty development initiatives such as workshops and technical seminars. Additional collaborative activities include the formation of an Ethics Committee, conducting medical camps, community health outreach programs, and organizing awareness initiatives such as free eye checkups and diagnostic screenings.
Benefits:
This partnership offers significant benefits including enhanced academic and research exposure, hands-on training in modern ophthalmic diagnostics, and real-time learning of clinical procedures. Students gain valuable industry-oriented skills, regulatory understanding, and practical experience that prepare them for careers in biomedical engineering and healthcare technology. Faculty members benefit from collaborative research

opportunities, professional development programs, and access to advanced ophthalmic tools and technologies. The joint initiatives also contribute to community welfare through health camps, screenings, and awareness programs, creating a broad social impact while strengthening institutional capabilities.
St. James Hospital Trust
29/01/2021
This MoU establishes a collaborative partnership between the two institutions to enhance academic learning, professional development, and community-oriented training. It focuses on knowledge exchange, joint research, skill development, staff collaboration, and providing students with real-world exposure through health education, workshops, field visits, and access to shared resources.
Focus:
The primary focus of this MoU is to strengthen public health education and awareness among students while promoting academic and teaching collaboration between the institutions. It aims to support joint research and training initiatives and foster professional behavior and community engagement. The MoU also emphasizes faculty–student exchange and mutual knowledge sharing to enhance academic and professional growth.
Scope:
The scope of this MoU includes conducting health education programs for students and facilitating the exchange of teaching materials, learning resources, and relevant academic literature. It further covers the organization of seminars, workshops, and conferences on mutually beneficial topics, along with collaboration on research and funded training programs. The agreement ensures students maintain ethical and professional behavior during community-based activities, provides reciprocal access to library facilities, and offers training and equipment support for student engagement. Additionally, the MoU outlines responsibilities for m

aintaining activity records, supporting community field visits, and enabling faculty from both institutions to deliver invited talks.
Benefits:
This MoU provides significant benefits, including enhanced academic exposure and access to enriched learning resources. It opens avenues for research, project involvement, and participation in funded programs. Students gain practical skills through real-world training and community interactions, while both students and faculty benefit from broadened professional development opportunities. The MoU also offers access to additional equipment, laboratories, and library facilities, promotes stronger collaboration through academic events, and prepares students to become ethical, industry-ready professionals.

Engineering Biomedical Students Association (EBSA)
The Engineering and Biomedical Students Association (EBSA) is the official student–faculty body of the Department of Biomedical Engineering. It serves as a collaborative platform that bridges students, faculty, and industry professionals to promote academic excellence, technical competence, and professional growth. EBSA organizes technical events, workshops, seminars, community outreach, and innovation challenges to nurture creativity and leadership among Biomedical Engineering students.
Vision
To empower students to become innovative, ethical, and socially responsible biomedical engineers capable of transforming healthcare through technology and research.
Mission:
Mission
To foster a vibrant learning environment that integrates engineering principles with medical sciences.
To enhance students’ technical, research, and professional skills through multidisciplinary exposure.
To encourage innovation, teamwork, and entrepreneurship in the field of biomedical engineering.
To establish strong connections between academia, healthcare institutions, and industry for collaborative learning and career development.
Association Objectives
To organi

ze workshops, technical talks, hands-on sessions, and industrial visits to bridge the gap between academics and the healthcare industry.
To increase awareness about emerging technologies, ethical practices, and societal impact of biomedical engineering.
To increase awareness about emerging technologies, ethical practices, and societal impact of biomedical engineering.
To encourage leadership, teamwork, and soft-skill development through student-managed events.
To foster entrepreneurship and innovation through incubation support, idea pitching, and start-up mentoring.
To engage in social and community outreach programs related to healthcare and technology.
To create a vibrant platform for biomedical engineering students to share ideas, innovations, and best practices.
To promote awareness and understanding of emerging trends in biomedical engineering.

Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids
Collaborative Learning
Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction
Alumni Interaction
Case Study
Mastery Learning
One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
ICT Enabled Teaching Learning Initiative
ICT-enabled webinar by Dr. Varghese Thomas exemplified innovative teaching methodologies that enrich the learning experience in anatomy and physiology. The session effectively utilized digital platforms to create an interactive and resource

-rich environment, aligning with the objective of enhanced student engagement and foundational knowledge reinforcement in biomedical sciences.
Download PDF
Innovative Teaching-Learning Report: Seminar Based Learning on Sustainability
This innovative teaching method aims to promote action-oriented learning among 3rd -semester biomedical engineering students, encouraging them to critically engage with real-world topics. By participating in seminar-based learning and preparing presentations on sustainability, students gain critical thinking skills and an awareness of current environmental issues, enhancing their understanding of global challenges.
Download PDF
Innovative Teaching Approach-Cross Over Learning
The primary objective of the visit was to gain insights into the medical device manufacturing industry, understand how advanced healthcare equipment is developed, and explore the integration of technology in medical applications.
Download PDF
Mock drill and Public Awareness Session 2025-2026 Academic Year
As part of BMT304 – Therapeutic Equipment's Sixth Semester BME students adopted an innovative teaching–learning approach by conducting a mock drill and public awareness session on AED ( Automatic External Defibrillator ) at the College Main Lobby, followed by a hands-on demonstration of AED, effectively linking theory with real-world emergency practice.

General Brief
Biotechnology
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
The Department of Biotechnology was established in 2002 and has been steadily growing ever since. We offer a B.Tech program with an intake of 60 students each year. Initially following the KTU curriculum, the department transitioned to an autonomous system in 2024, giving us more flexibility to improve and update our academic programs.
In addition to the undergraduate course, we also offer an M.Tech program in Industrial Biotechnology, which admits six students annually. The department is also offers  Ph.D. programs in biotechnology.
Our faculty members are dedicated to teaching, research, and student support. We focus on giving students a strong foundation in both theory and practical skills, preparing them for careers in research, industry, or further studies. The department aims to maintain a balanced, supportive learning environment where students can grow both academically and personally.
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.
Strong industry partnerships with excellent placement records.

Message
From the Head of Department
Join us in our journey of excellence, innovation, and impact as we shape the future of technology together
Dr.  Dhanya Gangadharan
Head of Department
BTE Department
Qualifications
Ph.D
13 Years Experience
Welcome to BTE Department
Over the past decade and more, the Department of Biotechnology at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology has established itself as a premier and most sought after college for pursuing Undergraduate Program (B Tech) in Biotechnology. A vibrant community of young, intellectual and talented citizens are moulded and nurtured here in the department. The department faculty is committed to undertake teaching and research as a passion with effective and fruitful methods of subject delivery towards etching the biotech engineers of tomorrow. The department is further building on the credible new outcome based methodologies of teaching and learning process to expand its departmental and institutional vision. Our department also addresses some complex issues pertaining to the area of human health and environment in society. An integral part of our endeavours has been our continuing science education and research towards new product and process development programmes. Commemorating the tenth year of the department an

d the college; Masters Programme (M Tech) in Industrial Biotechnology was initiated in 2013. This platform of higher learning is embodied with the view upgrading the students to play significant role in the Biotechnology and Pharmaceutical industries globally. The department will continue on its course to achieve excellence in biotechnology towards the realization of its mandate in the field.
Dr.  Dhanya Gangadharan
Head of Department, BTE Department

Program Educational Objectives
Biotechnology
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Educational Objectives (PEO)
Long-range goals that graduates are expected to achieve
Educational Objectives
On successful completion of the programme, graduates are expected to meet the following 3 educational objectives within a few years of graduation.
PEO 1
To be equipped with the engineering skills and technical concepts enabling them to take up careers in Biotechnology and allied fields globally.
PEO 2
To instill the passion for lifelong learning scientific discovery, allowing them to conduct research, communicate and innovate technologies keeping them motivated to take research and higher education.
PEO 3
To inculcate entrepreneurial and techno management skills along with professional and ethical responsibility empowering them to be responsible and socially aware citizens.

Program Outcomes
Biotechnology
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 10 program outcomes.
PO1
Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems
PO2
Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.
PO3
Design/ Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.
PO4
Conduct investigations of complex problems:  Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions
PO5
Modern Tool Usage:  Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resource

s, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations.
PO6
The Engineer and Society:  Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice.
PO7
Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development.
PO8
Ethics:  Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice.
PO9
Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions.
PO10
Lifelong Learning:  Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

Program Specific Outcomes
Biotechnology
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Specific Outcomes (PSO)
Domain-specific competencies unique to this programme
Specialization Outcomes
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 3 program specific outcomes.
PSO 1
To measure, model and manipulate properties of biological systems at the cellular and molecular level so as to produce sustainable products that address environmental and ethical standards
PSO 2
To formulate and execute quantitative and design- oriented analysis of biological systems with modern tools and techniques that generate new knowledge at the interface of engineering and biology
PSO 3
To transform as socially relevant biological engineers having a professional outlook that would enable them to work as a part of a team in an industrial, research or entrepreneurial set up and sustain a desire for higher learning and research.

Home
Departments
BTE
Department Labs
Biotechnology
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
BTE
Department Laboratories
State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology
The BTE department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education.
4
Labs
24/7
Wi-Fi
300+
Systems
Lab Details
MOLECULAR  BIOLOGY  LAB
Description: The laboratory is well equipped for creating hands on experience on fundamental techniques of molecular biology and genetic engineering. The laboratory is also equipped to perform experiments in Biochemistry.  The laboratory also has facilities for qualitative and quantitative analysis of biomolecules. Systems
Features:
Qualitative and quantitative analysis of biomolecules
CHEMICAL  ENGINEERING  LAB
Description: The department of biotechnology engineering offers students a unique learning experience with its Chemical engineering lab. The laboratory consists of a single room with 200 Sq.m area. An additional store room facility is also there to keep the consumables and glasswares which is attached to the lab. The laboratory is equipped with facilities to give students hands on experience on reaction engineering and process control and  unit-operation

s experiments focused on mass and heat transfer and fluid flow measurements among other chemical processes. Systems
Features:
Single room with 200 Sq.m area.
BIOPROCESS  ENGINEERING  LAB
Description: The lab is equipped with instruments to give a technical knowhow to students on microbial fermentation technology and downstream processing of biomolecules to strengthen the knowledge of the design and operation of a bioreactor and basic techniques of microbial cell disruption, enzyme studies and protein purification. Systems
Features:
Equipped with instruments to give a technical knowhow to students on microbial fermentation technology
MICRBIOLOGY  LAB
Description: The laboratory is well equipped to perform experiments on in vitro cultivation, quantification, preservation and characterization of microbes. The laboratory is also equipped with facilities for microbial analysis of food, water and soil. The microbiological experiments are performed in the laminar airflow chamber to maintain the biosafety. The lab also has facilities for sterilization and decontamination purpose. The laboratory also undertakes water and waste water testing as consultancy service which is approved by the Kerala State Pollution Control Board Systems
Features:
Equipped to perform experiments on in vitro cultivation
quantification
preservation and characterization of microbes

Department Association
Biotechnology
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
BEATS
To foster academic excellence, innovation, and professional development among biotechnology students through collaborative learning, research initiatives, industry engagement, and holistic skill-building that prepares them to address global challenges in healthcare, agriculture, and environmental sustainability.
Vision
To be a national centre of excellence in Biosciences and Bioengineering
Mission
1. Educate the next generation of engineers with a relevant curriculum that will enable
them to integrate biological sciences with engineering fundamentals that can create, model and modify biological systems for industrial applicability.
2. Encourage and facilitate research that would enable students to acquire new
knowledge that is industrially and socially relevant.
3. Train students to be leaders who value professional ethics and realize the need for
societal contribution and environmental sustainability in their work.
Association Objectives
Academic Excellence
To cultivate a culture of continuous learning and scholarly pursuit by providing students access to cutting-edge knowledge, expert guidance, and resources that enhance their understanding of biotechnology and related disciplines.
Practical Innovation
To bridge t

he gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application through hands-on workshops, laboratory sessions, projects, and collaborative experiments that encourage creative problem-solving and innovation.
Research and Development
To inspire and support student-led research initiatives, scientific investigations, and entrepreneurial ventures that contribute meaningful solutions to challenges in healthcare, agriculture, environmental sustainability, and industrial biotechnology.
Industry Integration
To establish strong connections with industry professionals, research institutions, and alumni through seminars, industrial visits, internships, and mentorship programs that enhance career readiness and professional networking.
Collaborative Community
To create an inclusive and supportive environment where students collaborate across disciplines, share knowledge, engage in peer learning, and build lasting relationships that foster personal and professional growth.
Leadership and Skill Development
To empower students with essential soft skills including communication, teamwork, project management, and leadership through event organization, technical competitions, and active participation in association activities.

Memorandums of Understanding
Strategic partnerships with industry and academic institutions
The department has established formal Memorandums of Understanding with leading industry partners and academic institutions to foster collaborative research, student internships, faculty development, and knowledge exchange.
5
Total MoUs
5
Focus Areas
100%
Active Status
0
Documented
Partnership Agreements
Agappe Diagnostics Ltd
28/11/2018
Focuses on strengthening industry–academia collaboration through training, product development, and skill enhancement in medical biotechnology and in-vitro diagnostics.
Focus:
Industry–academia interaction, student training and skill development
Scope:
Training by industry experts , joint product development and knowledge exchange,  Internship and placement opportunities
Benefits:
Improved practical skills, employability and exposure to industry practices
MoU with CARe KERALAM Ltd
15/03/2023
On 15 March 2023 the Department of Biotechnology signed MoU with CARe KERALAM Ltd  (Confederation for Ayurvedic Renaissance-Keralam Limited). It provides facilities for the standardization of Ayurvedic medicines. The company has facilities including raw material centre, clinical trials, analytical laborato

ry, production centre etc.
Focus:
Ayurvedic medicine
Scope:
Skill enhancement
Benefits:
Industry exposure and practical training
Vitaliz Bio science Pvt Ltd
23/10/2024
Aims to promote industry–academia collaboration by providing students with hands-on internship opportunities and offering faculty members specialized training aligned with industry practices and technological advancements.
Focus:
Industry-oriented student internships, faculty development through short-term professional training and exposure to practical tools, techniques, and real-world applications
Scope:
Internship programs for students lasting 4 to 6 months, tailored to VITALIZ’s operational needs and students’ academic requirements
Benefits:
Enhanced practical exposure and employability for students
MoU with  BiOZEEN
15/06/2023
Aimed at strengthening industry–academia collaboration by providing industrial biotechnology training to students and faculty, and by promoting joint research and development activities aligned with current industrial and research needs.
Focus:
Industrial training in biotechnology  and exposure to industry-standard infrastructure and practices
Scope:
Collaborative research and development projects, including public- and industry-funded projects
Benefits:
Enhanced hands-on industrial exposure for students  and Improved industry-readiness and skill development
MoU with Arjuna Naturals Pvt Ltd
15/03/2023
Department of Biotechnology proudly takes its next step towards establishing industry academia collaboration. On 15 March 2023 MoU was signed with Arjuna Natural Pvt Ltd. It is India’s leading manufacturer and exporter of botanical extracts for pharmaceuticals and nutraceuticals. As a CSR initiative they have also sponsored a smart classroom for the department which was inaugurate

d on the same day.
Focus:
Training
Scope:
Skill enhancement
Benefits:
Industry exposure

Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids
Collaborative Learning
Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction
Alumni Interaction
Case Study
Mastery Learning
One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
INNOVATIVE TLMs ACADEMIC YEAR 2022-23 & 2023-24
Various innovative Teaching and Learning Methods (TLMs)  are used across different courses, approaches such as problem-based learning, case-based activities, mini-projects, lab demonstrations, research-focused tasks, design thinking exercises, blended learning

through MOOCs, and industrial visits provide more quality education to the students. These methods emphasize practical understanding, creativity, and real-world application in engineering and biotechnology education.
Download PDF
INNOVATIVE TLMs ACADEMIC YEAR 2024-25
Innovative Teaching and Learning Methods this academic year features approaches such as problem-based learning, case-based learning, lab demonstrations, quizzes, design thinking activities, industrial visits, blended MOOC-based learning, mini-projects, and hands-on molecular biology sessions etc. These methods aim to build practical skills, conceptual clarity, and real-world application across technical subject

General Brief
Applied Science & Humanities
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
About the Department
The Department of Applied Science and Humanities (Formerly General Science Department) at Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology, established in 2002, stands as a beacon of excellence in technical education. Located in Kodakara, Thrissur, Kerala, our department is committed to providing world-class education and fostering innovation in the field of engineering.
Affiliated to KTU (APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University) and approved by AICTE.

Vision & Mission

Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Our Direction
Vision & Mission
Guiding principles that shape our commitment to academic excellence and innovation
Vision
Our Vision
Empower future innovators and leaders with a deep understanding of fundamentals and fostering meaningful contribution to the socio-technological landscape.
Our Mission
1 Prepare students for various engineering and technology programs by instilling a strong foundation in the areas of science and humanities with well experienced faculty.
2 Nurture practical, interpersonal and communication skills in students to prepare them industrially equipped.
3. Integrate ethical values and social issues with technical education

HOD Message

Message
From the Head of Department
Dr. Sukhila Krishnan
Head of Department
ASH Department
Qualifications
Ph.D. Polymer Science
Welcome to ASH Department
Department of Applied Science & Humanities has access to all streams of students irrespective of their branch of specialization. The department helps  them  in  moulding  the  right  attitude towards Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Economics and English . It also imparts training in life skills such as communication skills, soft skills and personality development skills  so  that  the students can face the challenges of the changing world with confidence.
Each discipline the department undertake has its own lab with the latest equipment and software. The department is equipped with competent and enthusiastic faculty members ready to adapt themselves with the rapidly changing technical world .
Dr. Sukhila Krishnan
Head of Department, ASH Department

Program Outcomes
Applied Science & Humanities
Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Program Outcomes (PO)
Engineering competencies and capabilities every graduate achieves
NBA / NAAC Accredited
On successful completion of the programme, graduates will demonstrate the following 11 program outcomes.
PO1
PO1: 	Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO2
PO2: Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.
PO3
3 PO3: Design/ Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.
PO4
PO4: Conduct investigations of complex problems:  Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions.
PO5
PO5:Modern Tool Usage:  Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex en

gineering activities with an understanding of the limitations.
PO6
PO6: The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental  aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability  with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment.  (WK1, WK5, and WK7).
PO7
PO11: Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for  i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies  and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)
PO8
PO8:  Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and  as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.
PO9
PO9: Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering  community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective  reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural,  language, and learning differences
PO10
PO10: Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of  engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to  one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in  multidisciplinary environments.
PO11
PO11: Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for  i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies  and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

Department Labs

Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
ASH
Department Laboratories
State-of-the-art computing facilities equipped with modern technology
The ASH department maintains a suite of fully equipped laboratories designed to support hands-on learning, research, and innovation. Each lab is fitted with the latest hardware and software to meet the demands of modern engineering education.
3
Labs
24/7
Wi-Fi
300+
Systems
ASH Lab Facilities
6 / 6
Lab Details
CHEMISTRY  LAB
Description: Chemistry Lab at Sahrdaya College of Engineering has a with a wide range of equipments to cater to the needs of Dr A.P.J Abdul Kalam Technological University’s first year curriculum. It enables the students to have a hands on training on various topics they study in “Chemistry” theory course. The lab is designed spaciously and well-ventilated to accommodate a capacity of 30 students at a time. Systems
Features:
The laboratory’s design includes ceramic tiles on all working tables
separate balance room and chemical store room.
MECHANICAL  ENGINEERING  WORKSHOP
Description: Mechanical Engineering Workshop is a place where students acquire knowledge on the operation of various processes involved in manufacturing and production. Systems
Features:
The Workshop Practice course makes students competent in handling practical work in engineering environment.
PHYSICS  LAB
Description:

Physics Lab at Sahrdaya College of Engineering has a with a wide range of equipments to cater to the needs of Dr A.P.J Abdul Kalam Technological University’s first year curriculum. It enables the students to have a hands on training on various topics they study in “Physics” theory course. The lab is designed spaciously and well-ventilated to accommodate a capacity of 30 students at a time. Systems
Features:
Here  students were taught to find the wavelength of monochromatic light like sodium vapour lamp and polychromatic light like Mercury vapour lamp using spectrometer. Using travelling microscope and sodium lamp
students were trained to measure the thickness of hair thin metal wire accurately. Students are trained to find the frequency of a tuning fork by Melde’s string
making them vibrating it in longitudinal and transverse mode.

Department Student Association
Fostering excellence in technical education and student development
Vision
To create a vibrant community of students who strive for academic excellence, technological innovation, and professional development while contributing meaningfully to society.
Mission
To provide a platform for students to enhance their technical skills, leadership abilities, and professional networks through workshops, seminars, competitions, and collaborative projects.
Association Objectives
Technical Excellence
Promote cutting-edge technical knowledge and practical skills
Industry Readiness
Bridge the academia-industry gap through practical exposure
Leadership Development
Foster leadership and organisational skills in students
Collaborative Learning
Encourage peer learning and knowledge sharing
Innovation & Research
Support research initiatives and innovative projects
Professional Network
Build connections with industry professionals and alumni

Sahrdaya College of Engineering & Technology
Innovative Teaching Learning Methods
Creating an open, creative and trustworthy learning environment
The innovative teaching-learning process is designed to create an open, creative, and trustworthy space for students to grow, take risks, and feel comfortable in their patterns of learning. Faculty initiate key actions to foster a more entrepreneurial and innovation-driven classroom experience.
Our Approach
We combine traditional pedagogy with modern digital tools to deliver outcomes-based education aligned with industry needs and KTU guidelines.
Teaching Methods Employed
Digital Learning
Classroom Quiz
Virtual Labs
Video Lectures
Low-Stakes Testing
Concept Mapping
Roleplay
Modern Tool Usage
Miniature Scale Models
MOOCs
Concept Tests
Audio-Visual Aids
Collaborative Learning
Industry Mentorship
Activity-Based Learning
Buzz Groups
Pair Interaction
Alumni Interaction
Case Study
Mastery Learning
One-To-One Communication
Website-Based Learning
TLM Documents & Resources
Video Lecturers
Students are provided with video lectures for some courses that they can access while they are away from the Institute Video lectures by the faculty members of our department are available on the YouTube Channel or via google classroom.
Classroom Quiz
A Classroom Quiz is a short assessment conducted during or after a lesson to eval

uate students’ understanding of the topic taught. This may be conducted via Etlab software or as a google quiz
Virtual teaching
Google Classroom Creating a Google classroom using google app. Students are made to join as members of the Google classroom. Lecture materials, Assignments, Quiz questions are posted in the online classroom.
Mastery Learning
Project-Based Learning Project-Based Learning focuses on developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills in the students. Its inquiry-based method of learning to solve the problems given as projects to the students is a style of active learning. Team activities and team projects help students to encounter their ideas to promote self-development and growth.
Digital Submissions
Etlab is a centralized and automated administration. It is a non-conventional method that supports collaborative learning, creating and delivering learning materials, generating reports, student notifications, etc. Digital submissions: Posting the teaching materials to facilitate blended learning

Vision
To provide comprehensive information resources and excellent services in support of the research, teaching and learning requisites of the Institution.
Mission
1.	To provide high quality services to the users by excelling in collecting, preserving and providing access to the best scholarly and educational resources to foster the growth of intellectual professionals, who shall employ it for the welfare of the society.
2.	To facilitate seamless access to robust and distinctive collections to the stakeholders.

Biography
Ms. Ragi C. Ravindran is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology, Kodakara, where she has been serving since 2019. She holds an M.Tech in Transportation Systems Engineering from the National Institute of Technology, Surathkal and a B.Tech in Civil Engineering from Govt. Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Technology, Kottayam. Her academic interests include transportation systems, sustainable pavements, and construction materials.

Biography
Dr. G.R.Gnana King, Senior Member IEEE is working as a Dean for PG and Doctoral Studies and Professor at Sahrdaya College of Engineering and Technology under APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University, Kerala. He received his Bachelor of Engineering degree in Electronics and communication Engineering, Master of Engineering degree in Applied Electronics and Doctorate in Information and communication Engineering from Anna University, India. He has around 16 years of experience in teaching and research and has been associated with many sponsored funded programmes by AICTE, KSCSTE, APJAKTU. He is currently supervising two research scholars in their Phd studies. He has around 100 research publications in peer reviewed journals, Books and International conferences to his credit and has delivered special lectures in Shot term training programmes and faculty development programmes. He received Excellent Faculty award in his academics and serving as an Editor and reviewer in various peer reviewed journals. His specific research interests include Bio Medical Image Processing, Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, Big Data Analysis and Digital Signal Processing.
